{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{座標一覧}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鳴神社'''（なるじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|和歌山県|Q131314}}{{jalink|和歌山市|Q200747}}{{jalink|鳴神 (和歌山市)|Q17209155|鳴神}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 * '''{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津彦命}}''' （はやあきつひこのみこと） - 左殿（向かって右殿）。 * '''{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津姫命}}''' （はやあきつひめのみこと） - 左殿（向かって右殿）。 * '''{{jalink|フトダマ|Q11059539|天太玉命}}''' （あめのふとだまのみこと） - 右殿（向かって左殿）。{{jalink|忌部氏|Q8189229}}祖神。 祭神2柱は水戸の神（港の神）である。古くは{{jalink|延享|Q1183728}}3年（{{jalink|1746年|Q7589}}）の『南紀神社録』において、速秋津彦・速秋津姫の2神を祭神とすると見える{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。しかしこれ以前の『南紀名勝志』には未詳とあり、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期には祭神が未詳の状態であったと見られている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 その後『{{jalink|紀伊続風土記|Q11604964}}』では、『延喜式』での鳴神社祭神が一座であることから速秋津彦・速秋津姫の2神祭神説を疑問視し、天太玉命を祭神とする説を掲げた{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。この推測は当地周辺を『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』に見える「忌部郷」の地に比定する説に基づくもので、同記では当地に居住した{{jalink|忌部氏|Q8189229}}（紀伊忌部）がその祖神・天太玉命を祀ったかという{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。加えて同記では、荒廃していた鳴神社が{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}4年（{{jalink|1719年|Q6902}}）に再興された際、速秋津彦・速秋津姫の2神に考定されたかという{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。ただし、紀伊忌部は天太玉命を祖とする忌部首の一族ではなく{{jalink|紀国造}}の一族であり、{{jalink|彦狭知命|Q135327097}}がその祖神であると『{{jalink|古語拾遺|Q531555}}』にある。また、紀国造の祖・{{jalink|多久豆魂命}}の別名である{{jalink|天石門別命}}{{jalink|中田憲信}}「望月」『諸系譜』第四冊。の父が{{jalink|天雷命}}という鳴神（{{jalink|雷神|Q12808082}}）とされ、『古屋家家譜』や『神別系譜』の大伴氏系図では天雷命に「紀伊国名草郡鳴神社（是也）」とある中田憲信『神別系譜』、{{jalink|東京国立博物館|Q653433}}デジタルライブラリー。。 天太玉命説に基づき、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}8年（{{jalink|1996年|Q2070}}）に忌部氏の本拠地である{{jalink|天太玉命神社|Q11442508}}（{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}忌部町）から改めて天太玉命の分霊が勧請され、本殿に合祀された。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 『紀伊名所図会』の引く社伝によれば、{{jalink|孝安天皇|Q312821}}（第6代）の頃、{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}の治水のために水門神である{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津日古命}}を祀るよう託宣があり、それによって祀られたのが鳴神社の創建であるという{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。社名「鳴」は、『{{jalink|紀伊続風土記|Q11604964}}』で「鳴水」「鳴滝」という谷から流れる水の意と推測されたように、川に関連した名であるとされる{{Sfn|鳴神社（神々）|1986年}}。 一方、{{jalink|承平 (日本)|Q1324874|承平}}年間（{{jalink|931年|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938年|Q24794}}）頃の『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』では、名草郡の{{jalink|郷|Q130458414}}として「忌部（いんべ）」の記載が見える。これは鳴神社南方の和歌山市井辺（いんべ）付近に比定されており、一帯は紀伊の{{jalink|忌部氏|Q8189229}}（紀伊忌部）の{{jalink|本貫|Q846706|本貫地}}であったとされる{{Harvtxt|和歌山県の地名|1983年}} 名草郡忌部郷項・神戸郷項。。また同書において「忌部」の次に「誰戸」の記載が見えるが、これは「神戸」の誤記で、「忌部神戸」すなわち忌部神の{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}（神社付属の民戸）を意味するとされる。関連して『新抄格勅符抄』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒では、忌部神の神戸20戸のうち紀伊国に10戸の記載が見える。鳴神社には『延喜式』四時祭相嘗祭条の「酒稲50束、神税」の記載から神戸があったことが確かであることから、これらの忌部神戸は鳴神社に付属したと見られ、鳴神社は紀伊忌部の氏神として奉斎されたと考えられている。またこれらの神戸は、鳴神社の鎮座する和歌山市鳴神周辺に分布したと推測される。 なお『{{jalink|古語拾遺|Q531555}}』では、紀伊忌部は{{jalink|彦狭知命|Q135327097}}の後裔であり、名草郡御木郷・麁香郷（いずれも『和名抄』なし）の2郷に居住して材木の貢納、宮殿・社殿造営を担ったと記されている『国史大辞典』忌部項、忌部氏項。。 === 概史 === 国史では{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）に従五位下勲八等から従四位下勲八等の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}昇叙の記事が記載されている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|紀伊国|Q864053}}{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}に「鳴神社 名神大 月次相嘗新嘗」として、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列するとともに{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|相嘗祭|Q11581944}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}で{{jalink|幣帛|Q11481885}}に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。紀伊国に名神大社は多いが、三祭で奉幣を受けたのは鳴神社と{{jalink|日前神宮・國懸神宮|Q888184|日前神社}}、{{jalink|日前神宮・國懸神宮|Q888184|国懸神社}}、{{jalink|伊太祁曽神社|Q11379271}}の4社のみであった。この4社については『{{jalink|令集解|Q11378321}}』神祇令『令集解』巻7（神祇令）仲冬条 上卯相嘗祭。でも「日前・国県須・伊太祁曽・鳴神」として、11月上卯の日の相嘗祭に際して奉幣社に列した旨の記載が見える{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}3年（{{jalink|1048年|Q19359}}）の収納米帳では、鳴神社田3段340歩が見える{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。また『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』では「正一位 鳴大神」と記載されている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}13年（{{jalink|1585年|Q6736}}）、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550}}による{{jalink|紀州征伐|Q10855721}}での{{jalink|太田城 (紀伊国)|Q10941625|太田城}}水攻めに際して、鳴神社含め付近は大いに荒廃したという。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}4年（{{jalink|1719年|Q6902}}）に社殿の造営がなされ、神田5反が寄進された{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}では{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|鳴神社（式内社）|1987年|p=54-56}}。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）、神社合祀令に伴い近隣にあった式内社の堅真音神社・香都知神社を境内に合祀した。 === 神階 === * 六国史における{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}奉叙の記録 ** {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）1月27日、従五位下勲八等から従四位下勲八等 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「鳴神」。 * 六国史以後 ** 正一位 （『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』） - 表記は「鳴大神」。 === 境内 === 本殿は2殿からなる。右殿（向かって左殿）に{{jalink|フトダマ|Q11059539|天太玉命}}が、左殿に{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津彦命・速秋津姫命}}が祀られている。 File:Naru-jinja honden.JPG|本殿 File:Naru-jinja haiden.JPG|拝殿 === 摂末社 === {{神社 |名称 = 堅真音神社・香都知神社 |画像 = [[File:Naru-jinja Katamaoto & Katsuchi-jinja.JPG|280px]]
社殿 |所在地 = |位置 = |祭神 = 堅真音神社：{{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|神吾田鹿葦津姫命}}
香都知神社：{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|軻遇突知命}} |神体 = |社格 = 堅真音神社：{{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
香都知神社：式内社（小）、旧村社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月12日|Q2920}}（両社とも）{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}} |神事 = }} === 堅真音神社 === '''堅真音神社'''（かたまおとじんじゃ、堅眞音神社）は、鳴神社の境内社で{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。現在は香都知神社と一社に併祀されている。 祭神は次の1柱。 * 神吾田鹿葦津姫命 （かみあだかあしつひめのみこと）{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}} *: {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|木花咲耶姫命}}の別名。 国史では、{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）に従五位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}に叙せられ、貞観7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）に官社に列し、貞観8年（{{jalink|866年|Q23872}}）に従四位下に叙せられた旨の記載が見える{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|紀伊国|Q864053}}{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}に「堅真音神社」と記載されて{{jalink|式内社}}に列しているほか、『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』にある「正一位 有真音大神」は堅真音神社に比定される{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。関連して『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』では名草郡の{{jalink|郷|Q130458414}}として「有真（ありま）」の記載が見え、和歌山市鳴神付近に比定されている{{Harvtxt|和歌山県の地名|1983年}} 有真郷項。。 堅真音神社は、元は鳴神社の東北約300メートルの地に鎮座した{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。故地には「堅真音神享保甲辰」として、甲辰年の{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}9年（{{jalink|1724年|Q6926}}）の銘と見られる碑が建てられている{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）4月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}}。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）の神社合祀令に伴い鳴神社境内に移転。その後和歌山市神前に分霊が勧請され、社殿が再建された（現・堅真音神社、{{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=堅真音神社（後裔社）}}）。 なお神階は次の通り。 * 六国史における{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}奉叙の記録（表記はいずれも「堅真音神」） ** {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）5月26日、正六位上から従五位上 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） ** 貞観7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）5月17日、官社に列す （『日本三代実録』） ** 貞観8年（{{jalink|866年|Q23872}}）閏3月13日、従五位上から従四位下 （『日本三代実録』） * 六国史以後 ** 正一位 （『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』） - 表記は「有真音大神」。 === 香都知神社 === '''香都知神社'''（かつちじんじゃ）は、鳴神社の境内社で{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。現在は堅真音神社と一社に併祀されている。 祭神は次の1柱。 * {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|軻遇突知命}} （かぐつちのみこと）{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|逆松社（和歌山市鳴神）
香都知神社旧鎮座地。}}]] 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|紀伊国|Q864053}}{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}に「香都知神社」と記載されて{{jalink|式内社}}に列しているが、写本によっては「鳴神末社」と注記されている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。また『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』では「従四位上 香都知神」と記載されている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 香都知神社は、元は鳴神社の東北約200メートルの地に鎮座した{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}（現在は小祠の逆松社が鎮座、{{Coord|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=逆松社（香都知神社旧地）}}）。{{jalink|紀州征伐|Q10855721}}の兵火により{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期には荒廃していたとされる{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。故地には「香都知神慶安庚寅」として、庚寅年の{{jalink|慶安|Q1146135}}3年（{{jalink|1650年|Q6951}}）の銘と見られる碑が建てられている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。{{jalink|延享|Q1183728}}3年（{{jalink|1746年|Q7589}}）の『南紀神社録』では香都知神社に関して「無宝殿建石碑」と記載されている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）4月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）の神社合祀令に伴い鳴神社境内に移転。 なお神階は次の通り。 * 六国史以後 ** 従四位上 （『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』） - 表記は「香都知神」。 === その他 === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|境内社}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳴武神社}}]] '''境内社''' * 天照大神宮その他の摂末社の記載は[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 鳴神社]（和歌山県神社庁）による。 * 春日社 * 夢神社 * 稲荷社 '''境外社''' * 鳴武神社（なるたけじんじゃ） *: 別称を「壺御前（つぼのごぜん）」。鳴神社の鳥居前に鎮座する。祭神の鳴武大明神は{{jalink|百済|Q28428}}の耆闍大王の第4王女であるといい、5世紀頃に百済から紀伊国に渡来して酒造を伝えたという。現在は石祠が残るのみであるが、古くは社殿があった。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|和歌山県|Q131314}}{{jalink|和歌山市|Q200747}}{{jalink|鳴神 (和歌山市)|Q17209155|鳴神}}1089 '''交通アクセス''' * {{jalink|和歌山電鐵|Q11417705}}（わかやま電鉄）{{jalink|和歌山電鐵貴志川線|Q6416586|貴志川線}} {{jalink|日前宮駅|Q11504968}}（徒歩約12分） * {{jalink|和歌山バス|Q11417290}} {{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|和歌山駅|Q1044107}}東口から鳴神線90・94系統 鳴神住宅前（徒歩約5分） '''周辺''' * {{jalink|日前神宮・國懸神宮|Q888184}} * 鳴神貝塚 - 国の史跡。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第23巻 南海道|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=田邊盛雄・吉田正純「鳴神社」|ref={{Harvid|鳴神社（式内社）|1987年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=田邊盛雄・吉田正純「香都知神社・堅真音神社」|ref={{Harvid|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref={{Harvid|和歌山県の地名|1983年}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「鳴神社」|ref={{Harvid|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「堅真音神社」|ref={{Harvid|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「香都知神社跡」|ref={{Harvid|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}|author=丸山顕徳|year=1986|chapter=鳴神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 6 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|鳴神社（神々）|1986年}}}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|忌部氏#忌部|Q8189229|忌部}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Naru-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 鳴神社] - torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''鳴神社'''（なるじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|和歌山県|Q131314}}{{jalink|和歌山市|Q200747}}{{jalink|鳴神 (和歌山市)|Q17209155|鳴神}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 現在の祭神は次の3柱神社由緒書。。 * '''{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津彦命}}''' （はやあきつひこのみこと） - 左殿（向かって右殿）。 * '''{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津姫命}}''' （はやあきつひめのみこと） - 左殿（向かって右殿）。 * '''{{jalink|フトダマ|Q11059539|天太玉命}}''' （あめのふとだまのみこと） - 右殿（向かって左殿）。{{jalink|忌部氏|Q8189229}}祖神。 祭神2柱は水戸の神（港の神）である。古くは{{jalink|延享|Q1183728}}3年（{{jalink|1746年|Q7589}}）の『南紀神社録』において、速秋津彦・速秋津姫の2神を祭神とすると見える{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。しかしこれ以前の『南紀名勝志』には未詳とあり、{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期には祭神が未詳の状態であったと見られている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 その後『{{jalink|紀伊続風土記|Q11604964}}』では、『延喜式』での鳴神社祭神が一座であることから速秋津彦・速秋津姫の2神祭神説を疑問視し、天太玉命を祭神とする説を掲げた{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。この推測は当地周辺を『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』に見える「忌部郷」の地に比定する説に基づくもので、同記では当地に居住した{{jalink|忌部氏|Q8189229}}（紀伊忌部）がその祖神・天太玉命を祀ったかという{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。加えて同記では、荒廃していた鳴神社が{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}4年（{{jalink|1719年|Q6902}}）に再興された際、速秋津彦・速秋津姫の2神に考定されたかという{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。ただし、紀伊忌部は天太玉命を祖とする忌部首の一族ではなく{{jalink|紀国造}}の一族であり、{{jalink|彦狭知命|Q135327097}}がその祖神であると『{{jalink|古語拾遺|Q531555}}』にある。また、紀国造の祖・{{jalink|多久豆魂命}}の別名である{{jalink|天石門別命}}{{jalink|中田憲信}}「望月」『諸系譜』第四冊。の父が{{jalink|天雷命}}という鳴神（{{jalink|雷神|Q12808082}}）とされ、『古屋家家譜』や『神別系譜』の大伴氏系図では天雷命に「紀伊国名草郡鳴神社（是也）」とある中田憲信『神別系譜』、{{jalink|東京国立博物館|Q653433}}デジタルライブラリー。。 天太玉命説に基づき、{{jalink|平成|Q234510}}8年（{{jalink|1996年|Q2070}}）に忌部氏の本拠地である{{jalink|天太玉命神社|Q11442508}}（{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|橿原市|Q496319}}忌部町）から改めて天太玉命の分霊が勧請され、本殿に合祀された。 === 歴史 === === 創建 === 『紀伊名所図会』の引く社伝によれば、{{jalink|孝安天皇|Q312821}}（第6代）の頃、{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}の治水のために水門神である{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津日古命}}を祀るよう託宣があり、それによって祀られたのが鳴神社の創建であるという{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。社名「鳴」は、『{{jalink|紀伊続風土記|Q11604964}}』で「鳴水」「鳴滝」という谷から流れる水の意と推測されたように、川に関連した名であるとされる{{Sfn|鳴神社（神々）|1986年}}。 一方、{{jalink|承平 (日本)|Q1324874|承平}}年間（{{jalink|931年|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938年|Q24794}}）頃の『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』では、名草郡の{{jalink|郷|Q130458414}}として「忌部（いんべ）」の記載が見える。これは鳴神社南方の和歌山市井辺（いんべ）付近に比定されており、一帯は紀伊の{{jalink|忌部氏|Q8189229}}（紀伊忌部）の{{jalink|本貫|Q846706|本貫地}}であったとされる{{Harvtxt|和歌山県の地名|1983年}} 名草郡忌部郷項・神戸郷項。。また同書において「忌部」の次に「誰戸」の記載が見えるが、これは「神戸」の誤記で、「忌部神戸」すなわち忌部神の{{jalink|神戸 (民戸)|Q11589711|神戸}}（神社付属の民戸）を意味するとされる。関連して『新抄格勅符抄』{{jalink|大同 (日本)|Q1373191|大同}}元年（{{jalink|806年|Q30021}}）牒では、忌部神の神戸20戸のうち紀伊国に10戸の記載が見える。鳴神社には『延喜式』四時祭相嘗祭条の「酒稲50束、神税」の記載から神戸があったことが確かであることから、これらの忌部神戸は鳴神社に付属したと見られ、鳴神社は紀伊忌部の氏神として奉斎されたと考えられている。またこれらの神戸は、鳴神社の鎮座する和歌山市鳴神周辺に分布したと推測される。 なお『{{jalink|古語拾遺|Q531555}}』では、紀伊忌部は{{jalink|彦狭知命|Q135327097}}の後裔であり、名草郡御木郷・麁香郷（いずれも『和名抄』なし）の2郷に居住して材木の貢納、宮殿・社殿造営を担ったと記されている『国史大辞典』忌部項、忌部氏項。。 === 概史 === 国史では{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）に従五位下勲八等から従四位下勲八等の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}昇叙の記事が記載されている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|紀伊国|Q864053}}{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}に「鳴神社 名神大 月次相嘗新嘗」として、{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}に列するとともに{{jalink|月次祭|Q11516161}}・{{jalink|相嘗祭|Q11581944}}・{{jalink|新嘗祭|Q11501518}}で{{jalink|幣帛|Q11481885}}に預かった旨が記載されている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。紀伊国に名神大社は多いが、三祭で奉幣を受けたのは鳴神社と{{jalink|日前神宮・國懸神宮|Q888184|日前神社}}、{{jalink|日前神宮・國懸神宮|Q888184|国懸神社}}、{{jalink|伊太祁曽神社|Q11379271}}の4社のみであった。この4社については『{{jalink|令集解|Q11378321}}』神祇令『令集解』巻7（神祇令）仲冬条 上卯相嘗祭。でも「日前・国県須・伊太祁曽・鳴神」として、11月上卯の日の相嘗祭に際して奉幣社に列した旨の記載が見える{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|永禄|Q1143341}}3年（{{jalink|1048年|Q19359}}）の収納米帳では、鳴神社田3段340歩が見える{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。また『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』では「正一位 鳴大神」と記載されている{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|天正|Q1145058}}13年（{{jalink|1585年|Q6736}}）、{{jalink|豊臣秀吉|Q187550}}による{{jalink|紀州征伐|Q10855721}}での{{jalink|太田城 (紀伊国)|Q10941625|太田城}}水攻めに際して、鳴神社含め付近は大いに荒廃したという。{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}4年（{{jalink|1719年|Q6902}}）に社殿の造営がなされ、神田5反が寄進された{{Sfn|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}では{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|鳴神社（式内社）|1987年|p=54-56}}。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）、神社合祀令に伴い近隣にあった式内社の堅真音神社・香都知神社を境内に合祀した。 === 神階 === * 六国史における{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}奉叙の記録 ** {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）1月27日、従五位下勲八等から従四位下勲八等 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） - 表記は「鳴神」。 * 六国史以後 ** 正一位 （『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』） - 表記は「鳴大神」。 === 境内 === 本殿は2殿からなる。右殿（向かって左殿）に{{jalink|フトダマ|Q11059539|天太玉命}}が、左殿に{{jalink|ハヤアキツヒコ・ハヤアキツヒメ|Q11326733|速秋津彦命・速秋津姫命}}が祀られている。 File:Naru-jinja honden.JPG|本殿 File:Naru-jinja haiden.JPG|拝殿 === 摂末社 === {{神社 |名称 = 堅真音神社・香都知神社 |画像 = [[File:Naru-jinja Katamaoto & Katsuchi-jinja.JPG|280px]]
社殿 |所在地 = |位置 = |祭神 = 堅真音神社：{{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|神吾田鹿葦津姫命}}
香都知神社：{{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|軻遇突知命}} |神体 = |社格 = 堅真音神社：{{jalink|式内社}}（小）、旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}
香都知神社：式内社（小）、旧村社 |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月12日|Q2920}}（両社とも）{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}} |神事 = }} === 堅真音神社 === '''堅真音神社'''（かたまおとじんじゃ、堅眞音神社）は、鳴神社の境内社で{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。現在は香都知神社と一社に併祀されている。 祭神は次の1柱。 * 神吾田鹿葦津姫命 （かみあだかあしつひめのみこと）{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}} *: {{jalink|コノハナノサクヤビメ|Q1781862|木花咲耶姫命}}の別名。 国史では、{{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）に従五位上の{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}に叙せられ、貞観7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）に官社に列し、貞観8年（{{jalink|866年|Q23872}}）に従四位下に叙せられた旨の記載が見える{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|紀伊国|Q864053}}{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}に「堅真音神社」と記載されて{{jalink|式内社}}に列しているほか、『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』にある「正一位 有真音大神」は堅真音神社に比定される{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。関連して『{{jalink|和名類聚抄|Q2702542}}』では名草郡の{{jalink|郷|Q130458414}}として「有真（ありま）」の記載が見え、和歌山市鳴神付近に比定されている{{Harvtxt|和歌山県の地名|1983年}} 有真郷項。。 堅真音神社は、元は鳴神社の東北約300メートルの地に鎮座した{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。故地には「堅真音神享保甲辰」として、甲辰年の{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}9年（{{jalink|1724年|Q6926}}）の銘と見られる碑が建てられている{{Sfn|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}7年（{{jalink|1874年|Q7761}}）4月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}}。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）の神社合祀令に伴い鳴神社境内に移転。その後和歌山市神前に分霊が勧請され、社殿が再建された（現・堅真音神社、{{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=堅真音神社（後裔社）}}）。 なお神階は次の通り。 * 六国史における{{jalink|神階|Q11591025}}奉叙の記録（表記はいずれも「堅真音神」） ** {{jalink|貞観 (日本)|Q1075635|貞観}}元年（{{jalink|859年|Q23897}}）5月26日、正六位上から従五位上 （『{{jalink|日本三代実録|Q1052981}}』） ** 貞観7年（{{jalink|865年|Q30350}}）5月17日、官社に列す （『日本三代実録』） ** 貞観8年（{{jalink|866年|Q23872}}）閏3月13日、従五位上から従四位下 （『日本三代実録』） * 六国史以後 ** 正一位 （『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』） - 表記は「有真音大神」。 === 香都知神社 === '''香都知神社'''（かつちじんじゃ）は、鳴神社の境内社で{{jalink|式内社}}。{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534|旧社格}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。現在は堅真音神社と一社に併祀されている。 祭神は次の1柱。 * {{jalink|カグツチ|Q1640658|軻遇突知命}} （かぐつちのみこと）{{Sfn|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|逆松社（和歌山市鳴神）
香都知神社旧鎮座地。}}]] 『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|神名帳}}では{{jalink|紀伊国|Q864053}}{{jalink|名草郡|Q11415547}}に「香都知神社」と記載されて{{jalink|式内社}}に列しているが、写本によっては「鳴神末社」と注記されている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。また『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』では「従四位上 香都知神」と記載されている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 香都知神社は、元は鳴神社の東北約200メートルの地に鎮座した{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}（現在は小祠の逆松社が鎮座、{{Coord|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=逆松社（香都知神社旧地）}}）。{{jalink|紀州征伐|Q10855721}}の兵火により{{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}初期には荒廃していたとされる{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。故地には「香都知神慶安庚寅」として、庚寅年の{{jalink|慶安|Q1146135}}3年（{{jalink|1650年|Q6951}}）の銘と見られる碑が建てられている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。{{jalink|延享|Q1183728}}3年（{{jalink|1746年|Q7589}}）の『南紀神社録』では香都知神社に関して「無宝殿建石碑」と記載されている{{Sfn|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年|p=370}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）4月、{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}に列した。明治40年（{{jalink|1907年|Q2048}}）の神社合祀令に伴い鳴神社境内に移転。 なお神階は次の通り。 * 六国史以後 ** 従四位上 （『{{jalink|紀伊国神名帳|Q17210008}}』） - 表記は「香都知神」。 === その他 === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|境内社}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|鳴武神社}}]] '''境内社''' * 天照大神宮その他の摂末社の記載は[http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 鳴神社]（和歌山県神社庁）による。 * 春日社 * 夢神社 * 稲荷社 '''境外社''' * 鳴武神社（なるたけじんじゃ） *: 別称を「壺御前（つぼのごぜん）」。鳴神社の鳥居前に鎮座する。祭神の鳴武大明神は{{jalink|百済|Q28428}}の耆闍大王の第4王女であるといい、5世紀頃に百済から紀伊国に渡来して酒造を伝えたという。現在は石祠が残るのみであるが、古くは社殿があった。 === 現地情報 === '''所在地''' * {{jalink|和歌山県|Q131314}}{{jalink|和歌山市|Q200747}}{{jalink|鳴神 (和歌山市)|Q17209155|鳴神}}1089 '''交通アクセス''' * {{jalink|和歌山電鐵|Q11417705}}（わかやま電鉄）{{jalink|和歌山電鐵貴志川線|Q6416586|貴志川線}} {{jalink|日前宮駅|Q11504968}}（徒歩約12分） * {{jalink|和歌山バス|Q11417290}} {{jalink|西日本旅客鉄道|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|和歌山駅|Q1044107}}東口から鳴神線90・94系統 鳴神住宅前（徒歩約5分） '''周辺''' * {{jalink|日前神宮・國懸神宮|Q888184}} * 鳴神貝塚 - 国の史跡。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 神社由緒書 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=式内社調査報告 第23巻 南海道|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=田邊盛雄・吉田正純「鳴神社」|ref={{Harvid|鳴神社（式内社）|1987年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=田邊盛雄・吉田正純「香都知神社・堅真音神社」|ref={{Harvid|香都知神社・堅真音神社（式内社）|1987年}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref={{Harvid|和歌山県の地名|1983年}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「鳴神社」|ref={{Harvid|鳴神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「堅真音神社」|ref={{Harvid|堅真音神社（平凡社）|1983年}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「香都知神社跡」|ref={{Harvid|香都知神社跡（平凡社）|1983年}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{jalink|谷川健一|Q2392596}}|author=丸山顕徳|year=1986|chapter=鳴神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 6 伊勢・志摩・伊賀・紀伊|publisher={{jalink|白水社|Q4346470}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|鳴神社（神々）|1986年}}}} === 関連項目 === * {{jalink|忌部氏#忌部|Q8189229|忌部}} === 外部リンク === {{Commonscat|Naru-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 鳴神社] - {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Foundation = (legendary) 6th {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Flowered construction|Q210684}}2 buildings |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Narujinja|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Narukami Shrine'' (Narujinja) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhiko Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhime Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}}''' (Amenofutodama no Mikoto) - Right shrine (facing left shrine). {{jalink|Ibe-san|Q8189229}}Ancestor God. The two deities enshrined are the gods of Mito (the gods of the port). As far back as the {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}), in the "Nanki Shrine Record", it appears that the two deities, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, are enshrined {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. However, the earlier "Nanki Meishoshi" states that it is unknown, and it is thought that the enshrined deity was unknown in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}{{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Later, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}},'' he questioned the theory that two gods were enshrined, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, since there was only one deity enshrined at Narukin Shrine in the ``Engishiki,'' and advocated the theory that Tentadama-no-Mikoto was the enshrined deity.{{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. This speculation is based on the theory that the area around this area is compared to the area of ``Ibe-go'' that appears in ``{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}'', and in the same text, it is said that a person lived in this area. {jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kiibe clan) is said to have enshrined its ancestral deity, Tenta Tama no Mikoto {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In addition, the same article states that when Naru Shrine, which had been in ruins, was rebuilt in 4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), two deities were considered: Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime. However, Kii-Ibe is not a family of Inebekushi whose ancestor is Tenta Tama-no-Mikoto, but a family of {{jalink|Kikokuzō}}, and {{jalink|Hikosa Chi-no-Mikoto|Q135327097}} is its ancestor deity, according to ``{{jalink|Kogo Shui|Q531555}}.'' Also, the fourth book of ``Mochizuki'' and ``Sho Keiki,'' which is another name for the founder of Kikuni-zukuri, {{jalink|Takuma Tama no Mikoto}}. 's father is said to be a Narukami ({{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}) called {{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}, and in the Otomo clan genealogy in "Furuya family family history" and "Kamibetsu genealogy," Tenrai no Mikoto is said to be ``Narukami Shrine (Koreya), Nagusa district, Kii province.''Keninobu Nakata "Shinbetsu Genealogy", {{jalink|Tokyo National Museum|Q653433}} Digital Library. .Based on the Tentaitama no Mikoto theory, in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}), {{jalink|Tentadama no Mikoto Shrine|Q11] is the stronghold of the Inabe clan. 442508}} ({{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}Ibe Town), the spirit of Tentadama no Mikoto was once again solicited and enshrined in the main shrine.. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine biography cited in "Ki Meisho Zue", around the time of {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} (sixth generation), the god {{jalink|Ha It is said that there was an oracle to enshrine Yaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and Naru-jinja Shrine was founded as a result of this enshrinement. The company name ``Naru'' is said to be a name related to a river, as it is assumed in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'' that it means water flowing from a valley called ``Narumizu'' or ``Narutaki''. {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Gods)|1986}}On the other hand, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) link|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", you can see the entry "Inbe" as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District.. This is believed to be near Inbe, Wakayama City, south of Narukin Shrine, and the area is said to have been the {{jalink|honkan|Q846706|honkanchi}} of {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kii Inbe) in Kii. Nagusa District, Ibe-go section, Kobe-go section. . Also, in the same book, ``Nabe'' appears next to ``Ibe'', but this is a misprint of ``Kobe'', and is thought to mean ``Ibe Kobe'', that is, the god of Inbe (a private house attached to a shrine). Relatedly, in the ``Shinshokakuchofusho'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), there are mentions of 10 of the 20 households of Inabe deity in Kobe . It is certain that Kobe was located at Naru-jinja Shrine from the description of ``50 bunches of sake rice, divine tax'' in the Shijiki-sai Ainosai article of the ``Engishiki'', so it is thought that these Inabe-Kobe were attached to Naru-jinja Shrine, and Naru-jinja Shrine was enshrined as the guardian deity of Kii Inabe. It is also assumed that these Kobes were distributed around Narukami, Wakayama City, where Narukami Shrine is located .In addition, in "{{jalink|Kogo shui|Q531555}}", Kiibe is a descendant of {{jalink|Hikosato no Mikoto|Q135327097}}, and is the ruler of Nagusa District. It is written that he lived in Kigo and Arakago (neither of which have "Wamyosho") and was responsible for paying tribute of lumber and building palaces and shrines. . === Outline === In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} from the 8th grade of Jugoi to the 8th grade of Jushii (Junior 4th rank and lower rank) in the first year ({{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nagusa District|Q11415547}} has "Narujin Shrine Meishin University It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Ainame Shinnai'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Ainamesai|Q11581944}}・It is written that it was deposited in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} at {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Narukinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. There are many famous shrines in Kii Province, but the ones that received dedications at the three festivals are Narukinja Shrine, {{jalink|Maezen Shrine, Kunikake Shrine|Q888184|Maejin Shrine}}, {{ja There were only four shrines: link|Mae-mae Jingu/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|Kunikake Shrine}} and {{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}}. Regarding these four companies, please refer to "{{jalink|Reishūka|Q11378321}}" Jingirei"Reishūkai" Volume 7 (Jingirei) Nakafuyujo Kamiusaōsai. Also, there is a description that it was listed at Hoheisha Shrine on the occasion of the Aijo Festival on Kami U no Hi in November as ``Mae, Kokukensu, Itaukeso, Narukami'' {{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In the storage rice book of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}3 ({{jalink|1048|Q19359}}), 340 steps of Narukami Shrine rice fields can be seen {{Sfn|Narukin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'', it is written as ``Shoichii Naru-Okami'' {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 13 ({{jalink|1585|Q6736}}), {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}'s {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}} {{jalink|Ota Castle (Ki Province)|Q10941625|Ota Castle}}It is said that during the water attack, the surrounding area, including Narukin Shrine, was greatly devastated. The shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), and five Kanda shrines were donated {{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. In the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines, the nearby Shikinai shrines Kenmaon Shrine and Katsuchi Shrine were enshrined within the shrine grounds. === God level === * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} January 27, 1985 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - The notation is "Narukami". * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{jalink|Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}'') - Written as "Narutokami". === Precincts === The main hall consists of two halls. {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} is enshrined in the right shrine (facing the left shrine), and {{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko/Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto/Hayaakitsuhime-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined in the left shrine. File:Naru-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Naru-jinja haiden.JPG|House of worship === Setsusha === {{Shrine |Name = Kenmaon Shrine/Katsuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Naru-jinja Katamaoto & Katsuchi-jinja.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = |Position = |Enshrined deity = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Konohananosakuyabime|Q1781862|Kamigota Shikaashitsuhime no Mikoto}}
Katsuchi Shrine: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kibutsuchi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} (small), former {{jalink|mura-sha|Q134917284}}
Katsuchi Shrine: Shikinai-sha (small), former village-sha |Soken = |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} (Both shrines) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} |Shinto ritual = }} === Kenmaon Shrine === ``Katamaoto Shrine'' (Katamanoto Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukin Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kotsuchi Shrine. The following deities are enshrined: * Kamiadaka Ashitsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} *: Another name for {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}, he was conferred {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}}, which is the upper rank of Jugoi, and in the seventh year of Jogan ({{jalink|865 In 1983|Q30350}}), it was ranked as a government shrine, and in the 8th year of Jogan ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), there is a record that it was conferred the rank of Junior Fourth Rank and Lower.{{Sfn|Kanshin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}."{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink |Nagusa District|Q11415547}} is described as "Kanmaon Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, and in "{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}" it is listed as "Shoichii Shrine". ``Arimaon Okami'' is compared to Kenmaon Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Relatedly, in "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", "Arima" is mentioned as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District, and it is determined that it is near Narukami, Wakayama City. . Kenmaon Shrine was originally located approximately 300 meters northeast of Naru Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsune Shrine Kyoho Kotatsu'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}) {{Sfn|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} April 7th ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q71253 4}}, {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} listed as {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. Later, a branch spirit was summoned to the shrine in Wakayama City, and the shrine building was rebuilt (currently Kenma-on Shrine, {{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Kanma-on Shrine (descendant shrine)}}). The shrine is as follows. * Records of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms (all written as "Kanma Ongami") ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} May 26th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to upper junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') ** On May 17, 7th year of Jogan era ({{jalink|865 years|Q30350}}), he was listed as a government shrine (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') ** Leap March 13, 8th year of the Jogan era ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii ("{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}") - Written as "Arimaon Okami". === Katsuchi Shrine === '''Katsuchi Shrine'' (Katsuchi Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukinja Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kenmaon Shrine. The following deities are enshrined. * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Encounter with Tsuchi no Mikoto}} (Kagutsuchi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sakamatsu Shrine (Narukami, Wakayama City)
The former enshrinement site of Katsuchi Shrine. }}]] "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11 415547}}, it is written as ``Katsuchi Shrine'' and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, but in some manuscripts it is annotated as ``Narukami Sue Shrine'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Katsuchijin'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Kotsuchi Shrine was originally located about 200 meters northeast of Naruko Shrine. rd|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Gakamatsu Shrine (formerly Katsuchi Shrine)}}). {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}} It is said that it was in ruins in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} due to the military fire of the {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsuchijin Keian Kotora'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Keian|Q1146135}}3 ({{jalink|1650|Q6951}}) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) "Nanki Jinja Roku" describes Kotsuchi Shrine as "Muhoden erected stone monument" {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In April of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern company ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. The shrine is as follows. * After Six Kingdoms History** Junior Fourth Rank Upper (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Katsuchijin''. === Others === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Narutake Shrine}}]] '''Precinct shrine''' * Amaterasu Daijingu ShrineThe description of other shrines is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narukin Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Kasugasha * Yume Shrine * Inarisha ``Kingaisha'''' * Narutake Shrine *: Another name is "Tsubo Gozen" . It is enshrined in front of the torii gate of Naru Shrine. The enshrined deity, Narumu Daimyojin, is said to be the fourth princess of King Kija of {{jalink|Baekje|Q28428}}, and is said to have come from Baekje to Kii Province around the 5th century and introduced sake brewing. Currently, only the stone shrine remains, but in ancient times there was a shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}1089 '''Access''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway|Q11417705}} (Wakayama Electric Railway) {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway Kishigawa Line|Q6416586|Kishigawa Line}} {{jalink|Naemiya Station|Q11504968}} (about 12 minutes walk) * {{jalink|Wakayama Bus|Q11417290}} {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|Wakayama Station|Q1044107}} From the east exit, Narukami Line 90/94 Narukami Jutaku-mae (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} * Narukami Shell Midden - National historic site. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Original text''' {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist|2}} === References === * Shrine history book * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Research Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=Morio Tanabe and Masazumi Yoshida "Naru Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Naru Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}}}, {{Wikici te|reference=Morio Tanabe and Masazumi Yoshida "Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987}}}}. * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 31 Place names in Wakayama prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref={{Harvid|Place names in Wakayama prefecture|1983}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Naru Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Kanmaon Shrine"|ref={ {Harvid|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=``Katsuchi Shrine Ruins''|ref={{Harvid|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}.* {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Kentori Maruyama|year=1986|chapter=Narujinja|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 6 Ise/Shima/Iga/Kii|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|Narujinja (Gods)|1986}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Ibe clan#Ibe|Q8189229|Ibe}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Naru-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narujinja Shrine] - Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520403.html Naru Shrine], [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520801.html Kenmaon Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection" {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Kanmaon Shrine |1-1=Wakayama City Shrine |1-2=Shikiuchi Shrine in Kii Province |1-3=Old village shrine in Wakayama Prefecture |redirect2=Katsuchi Shrine |2-1=Shikiuchi Shrine in Kii Province |2-2=Old village shrine in Wakayama Prefecture }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Narushinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naru Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naru Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名なるしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:忌部氏|社なるしんしや]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

== street address (P6375) == * {'text': '和歌山県和歌山市鳴神1089', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Naru-jinja}} [[JA:鳴神社]] {{wikidata link|Q17209196}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Naruko Shrine |Image = [[File:Naru-jinja shaden.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}1089 |Location = {{Coord|34|13|40.19|N|135|12|40.79|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Narushinsha}} |Enshrined deity = {{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Foundation = (legendary) 6th {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Flowered construction|Q210684}}2 buildings |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Narujinja|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Narukami Shrine'' (Narujinja) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhiko Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhime Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}}''' (Amenofutodama no Mikoto) - Right shrine (facing left shrine). {{jalink|Ibe-san|Q8189229}}Ancestor God. The two deities enshrined are the gods of Mito (the gods of the port). As far back as the {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}), in the "Nanki Shrine Record", it appears that the two deities, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, are enshrined {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. However, the earlier "Nanki Meishoshi" states that it is unknown, and it is thought that the enshrined deity was unknown in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}{{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Later, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}},'' he questioned the theory that two gods were enshrined, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, since there was only one deity enshrined at Narukin Shrine in the ``Engishiki,'' and advocated the theory that Tentadama-no-Mikoto was the enshrined deity.{{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. This speculation is based on the theory that the area around this area is compared to the area of ``Ibe-go'' that appears in ``{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}'', and in the same text, it is said that a person lived in this area. {jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kiibe clan) is said to have enshrined its ancestral deity, Tenta Tama no Mikoto {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In addition, the same article states that when Naru Shrine, which had been in ruins, was rebuilt in 4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), two deities were considered: Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime. However, Kii-Ibe is not a family of Inebekushi whose ancestor is Tenta Tama-no-Mikoto, but a family of {{jalink|Kikokuzō}}, and {{jalink|Hikosa Chi-no-Mikoto|Q135327097}} is its ancestor deity, according to ``{{jalink|Kogo Shui|Q531555}}.'' Also, the fourth book of ``Mochizuki'' and ``Sho Keiki,'' which is another name for the founder of Kikuni-zukuri, {{jalink|Takuma Tama no Mikoto}}. 's father is said to be a Narukami ({{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}) called {{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}, and in the Otomo clan genealogy in "Furuya family family history" and "Kamibetsu genealogy," Tenrai no Mikoto is said to be ``Narukami Shrine (Koreya), Nagusa district, Kii province.''Keninobu Nakata "Shinbetsu Genealogy", {{jalink|Tokyo National Museum|Q653433}} Digital Library. .Based on the Tentaitama no Mikoto theory, in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}), {{jalink|Tentadama no Mikoto Shrine|Q11] is the stronghold of the Inabe clan. 442508}} ({{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}Ibe Town), the spirit of Tentadama no Mikoto was once again solicited and enshrined in the main shrine.. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine biography cited in "Ki Meisho Zue", around the time of {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} (sixth generation), the god {{jalink|Ha It is said that there was an oracle to enshrine Yaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and Naru-jinja Shrine was founded as a result of this enshrinement. The company name ``Naru'' is said to be a name related to a river, as it is assumed in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'' that it means water flowing from a valley called ``Narumizu'' or ``Narutaki''. {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Gods)|1986}}On the other hand, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) link|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", you can see the entry "Inbe" as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District.. This is believed to be near Inbe, Wakayama City, south of Narukin Shrine, and the area is said to have been the {{jalink|honkan|Q846706|honkanchi}} of {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kii Inbe) in Kii. Nagusa District, Ibe-go section, Kobe-go section. . Also, in the same book, ``Nabe'' appears next to ``Ibe'', but this is a misprint of ``Kobe'', and is thought to mean ``Ibe Kobe'', that is, the god of Inbe (a private house attached to a shrine). Relatedly, in the ``Shinshokakuchofusho'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), there are mentions of 10 of the 20 households of Inabe deity in Kobe . It is certain that Kobe was located at Naru-jinja Shrine from the description of ``50 bunches of sake rice, divine tax'' in the Shijiki-sai Ainosai article of the ``Engishiki'', so it is thought that these Inabe-Kobe were attached to Naru-jinja Shrine, and Naru-jinja Shrine was enshrined as the guardian deity of Kii Inabe. It is also assumed that these Kobes were distributed around Narukami, Wakayama City, where Narukami Shrine is located .In addition, in "{{jalink|Kogo shui|Q531555}}", Kiibe is a descendant of {{jalink|Hikosato no Mikoto|Q135327097}}, and is the ruler of Nagusa District. It is written that he lived in Kigo and Arakago (neither of which have "Wamyosho") and was responsible for paying tribute of lumber and building palaces and shrines. . === Outline === In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} from the 8th grade of Jugoi to the 8th grade of Jushii (Junior 4th rank and lower rank) in the first year ({{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nagusa District|Q11415547}} has "Narujin Shrine Meishin University It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Ainame Shinnai'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Ainamesai|Q11581944}}・It is written that it was deposited in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} at {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Narukinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. There are many famous shrines in Kii Province, but the ones that received dedications at the three festivals are Narukinja Shrine, {{jalink|Maezen Shrine, Kunikake Shrine|Q888184|Maejin Shrine}}, {{ja There were only four shrines: link|Mae-mae Jingu/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|Kunikake Shrine}} and {{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}}. Regarding these four companies, please refer to "{{jalink|Reishūka|Q11378321}}" Jingirei"Reishūkai" Volume 7 (Jingirei) Nakafuyujo Kamiusaōsai. Also, there is a description that it was listed at Hoheisha Shrine on the occasion of the Aijo Festival on Kami U no Hi in November as ``Mae, Kokukensu, Itaukeso, Narukami'' {{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In the storage rice book of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}3 ({{jalink|1048|Q19359}}), 340 steps of Narukami Shrine rice fields can be seen {{Sfn|Narukin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'', it is written as ``Shoichii Naru-Okami'' {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 13 ({{jalink|1585|Q6736}}), {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}'s {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}} {{jalink|Ota Castle (Ki Province)|Q10941625|Ota Castle}}It is said that during the water attack, the surrounding area, including Narukin Shrine, was greatly devastated. The shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), and five Kanda shrines were donated {{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. In the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines, the nearby Shikinai shrines Kenmaon Shrine and Katsuchi Shrine were enshrined within the shrine grounds. === God level === * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} January 27, 1985 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - The notation is "Narukami". * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{jalink|Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}'') - Written as "Narutokami". === Precincts === The main hall consists of two halls. {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} is enshrined in the right shrine (facing the left shrine), and {{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko/Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto/Hayaakitsuhime-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined in the left shrine. File:Naru-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Naru-jinja haiden.JPG|House of worship === Setsusha === {{Shrine |Name = Kenmaon Shrine/Katsuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Naru-jinja Katamaoto & Katsuchi-jinja.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = |Position = |Enshrined deity = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Konohananosakuyabime|Q1781862|Kamigota Shikaashitsuhime no Mikoto}}
Katsuchi Shrine: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kibutsuchi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} (small), former {{jalink|mura-sha|Q134917284}}
Katsuchi Shrine: Shikinai-sha (small), former village-sha |Soken = |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} (Both shrines) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} |Shinto ritual = }} === Kenmaon Shrine === ``Katamaoto Shrine'' (Katamanoto Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukin Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kotsuchi Shrine. The following deities are enshrined: * Kamiadaka Ashitsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} *: Another name for {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}, he was conferred {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}}, which is the upper rank of Jugoi, and in the seventh year of Jogan ({{jalink|865 In 1983|Q30350}}), it was ranked as a government shrine, and in the 8th year of Jogan ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), there is a record that it was conferred the rank of Junior Fourth Rank and Lower.{{Sfn|Kanshin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}."{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink |Nagusa District|Q11415547}} is described as "Kanmaon Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, and in "{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}" it is listed as "Shoichii Shrine". ``Arimaon Okami'' is compared to Kenmaon Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Relatedly, in "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", "Arima" is mentioned as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District, and it is determined that it is near Narukami, Wakayama City. . Kenmaon Shrine was originally located approximately 300 meters northeast of Naru Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsune Shrine Kyoho Kotatsu'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}) {{Sfn|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} April 7th ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q71253 4}}, {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} listed as {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. Later, a branch spirit was summoned to the shrine in Wakayama City, and the shrine building was rebuilt (currently Kenma-on Shrine, {{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Kanma-on Shrine (descendant shrine)}}). The shrine is as follows. * Records of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms (all written as "Kanma Ongami") ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} May 26th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to upper junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') ** On May 17, 7th year of Jogan era ({{jalink|865 years|Q30350}}), he was listed as a government shrine (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') ** Leap March 13, 8th year of the Jogan era ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii ("{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}") - Written as "Arimaon Okami". === Katsuchi Shrine === '''Katsuchi Shrine'' (Katsuchi Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukinja Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kenmaon Shrine. The following deities are enshrined. * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Encounter with Tsuchi no Mikoto}} (Kagutsuchi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sakamatsu Shrine (Narukami, Wakayama City)
The former enshrinement site of Katsuchi Shrine. }}]] "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11 415547}}, it is written as ``Katsuchi Shrine'' and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, but in some manuscripts it is annotated as ``Narukami Sue Shrine'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Katsuchijin'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Kotsuchi Shrine was originally located about 200 meters northeast of Naruko Shrine. rd|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Gakamatsu Shrine (formerly Katsuchi Shrine)}}). {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}} It is said that it was in ruins in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} due to the military fire of the {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsuchijin Keian Kotora'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Keian|Q1146135}}3 ({{jalink|1650|Q6951}}) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) "Nanki Jinja Roku" describes Kotsuchi Shrine as "Muhoden erected stone monument" {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In April of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern company ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. The shrine is as follows. * After Six Kingdoms History** Junior Fourth Rank Upper (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Katsuchijin''. === Others === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Narutake Shrine}}]] '''Precinct shrine''' * Amaterasu Daijingu ShrineThe description of other shrines is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narukin Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Kasugasha * Yume Shrine * Inarisha ``Kingaisha'''' * Narutake Shrine *: Another name is "Tsubo Gozen" . It is enshrined in front of the torii gate of Naru Shrine. The enshrined deity, Narumu Daimyojin, is said to be the fourth princess of King Kija of {{jalink|Baekje|Q28428}}, and is said to have come from Baekje to Kii Province around the 5th century and introduced sake brewing. Currently, only the stone shrine remains, but in ancient times there was a shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}1089 '''Access''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway|Q11417705}} (Wakayama Electric Railway) {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway Kishigawa Line|Q6416586|Kishigawa Line}} {{jalink|Naemiya Station|Q11504968}} (about 12 minutes walk) * {{jalink|Wakayama Bus|Q11417290}} {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|Wakayama Station|Q1044107}} From the east exit, Narukami Line 90/94 Narukami Jutaku-mae (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} * Narukami Shell Midden - National historic site. === Footnotes === {{Footnote help}} '''Original text''' {{reflist|group="hara"}} '''Source''' {{reflist|2}} === References === * Shrine history book * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=Shikinaisha Research Group|author=|chapter=|year=1987|title=Shikinaisha Research Report Volume 23 Nankaido|publisher={{jalink|Kogakkan University|Q1797584}}Publishing Department|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=Morio Tanabe and Masazumi Yoshida "Naru Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Naru Shrine (Shikinai Shrine)|1987}}}}, {{Wikici te|reference=Morio Tanabe and Masazumi Yoshida "Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987}}}}. * {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=|title={{jalink|Japanese historical place name system|Q17213509}} 31 Place names in Wakayama prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=9784582490312|ref={{Harvid|Place names in Wakayama prefecture|1983}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference="Naru Shrine"|ref={{Harvid|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference="Kanmaon Shrine"|ref={ {Harvid|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=``Katsuchi Shrine Ruins''|ref={{Harvid|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983}}}}.* {{Cite book|Japanese book|editor={{jalink|Kenichi Tanigawa|Q2392596}}|author=Kentori Maruyama|year=1986|chapter=Narujinja|title=Japanese Gods -Shrines and Sacred Sites- 6 Ise/Shima/Iga/Kii|publisher={{jalink|Hakusuisha|Q4346470}}|isbn=456002216X|ref={{Harvid|Narujinja (Gods)|1986}}}} === Related items === * {{jalink|Ibe clan#Ibe|Q8189229|Ibe}} === External link === {{Commonscat|Naru-jinja}} * [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narujinja Shrine] - Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Office * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520403.html Naru Shrine], [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/520801.html Kenmaon Shrine] - Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program "Shinto and Shrine Historical Materials Collection" {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Redirect belongs to category |redirect1=Kanmaon Shrine |1-1=Wakayama City Shrine |1-2=Shikiuchi Shrine in Kii Province |1-3=Old village shrine in Wakayama Prefecture |redirect2=Katsuchi Shrine |2-1=Shikiuchi Shrine in Kii Province |2-2=Old village shrine in Wakayama Prefecture }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Narushinsiya}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Naru Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Naru Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kii Province]] [[Category:和歌山市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:紀伊国の式内社|名なるしんしや]] [[Category:和歌山県の旧村社]] [[Category:忌部氏|社なるしんしや]] [[Category:Need translation]] [[Category:kkkkkkkkkkkkkk]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qq]]

Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Foundation = (legendary) 6th {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Flowered construction|Q210684}}2 buildings |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Narujinja|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Narukami Shrine'' (Narujinja) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhiko Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhime Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}}''' (Amenofutodama no Mikoto) - Right shrine (facing left shrine). {{jalink|Ibe-san|Q8189229}}Ancestor God. The two deities enshrined are the gods of Mito (the gods of the port). As far back as the {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}), in the "Nanki Shrine Record", it appears that the two deities, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, are enshrined {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. However, the earlier "Nanki Meishoshi" states that it is unknown, and it is thought that the enshrined deity was unknown in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}{{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Later, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}},'' he questioned the theory that two gods were enshrined, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, since there was only one deity enshrined at Narukin Shrine in the ``Engishiki,'' and advocated the theory that Tentadama-no-Mikoto was the enshrined deity.{{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. This speculation is based on the theory that the area around this area is compared to the area of ``Ibe-go'' that appears in ``{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}'', and in the same text, it is said that a person lived in this area. {jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kiibe clan) is said to have enshrined its ancestral deity, Tenta Tama no Mikoto {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In addition, the same article states that when Naru Shrine, which had been in ruins, was rebuilt in 4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), two deities were considered: Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime. However, Kii-Ibe is not a family of Inebekushi whose ancestor is Tenta Tama-no-Mikoto, but a family of {{jalink|Kikokuzō}}, and {{jalink|Hikosa Chi-no-Mikoto|Q135327097}} is its ancestor deity, according to ``{{jalink|Kogo Shui|Q531555}}.'' Also, the fourth book of ``Mochizuki'' and ``Sho Keiki,'' which is another name for the founder of Kikuni-zukuri, {{jalink|Takuma Tama no Mikoto}}. 's father is said to be a Narukami ({{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}) called {{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}, and in the Otomo clan genealogy in "Furuya family family history" and "Kamibetsu genealogy," Tenrai no Mikoto is said to be ``Narukami Shrine (Koreya), Nagusa district, Kii province.''Keninobu Nakata "Shinbetsu Genealogy", {{jalink|Tokyo National Museum|Q653433}} Digital Library. .Based on the Tentaitama no Mikoto theory, in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}), {{jalink|Tentadama no Mikoto Shrine|Q11] is the stronghold of the Inabe clan. 442508}} ({{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}Ibe Town), the spirit of Tentadama no Mikoto was once again solicited and enshrined in the main shrine.. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine biography cited in "Ki Meisho Zue", around the time of {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} (sixth generation), the god {{jalink|Ha It is said that there was an oracle to enshrine Yaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and Naru-jinja Shrine was founded as a result of this enshrinement. The company name ``Naru'' is said to be a name related to a river, as it is assumed in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'' that it means water flowing from a valley called ``Narumizu'' or ``Narutaki''. {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Gods)|1986}}On the other hand, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) link|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", you can see the entry "Inbe" as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District.. This is believed to be near Inbe, Wakayama City, south of Narukin Shrine, and the area is said to have been the {{jalink|honkan|Q846706|honkanchi}} of {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kii Inbe) in Kii. Nagusa District, Ibe-go section, Kobe-go section. . Also, in the same book, ``Nabe'' appears next to ``Ibe'', but this is a misprint of ``Kobe'', and is thought to mean ``Ibe Kobe'', that is, the god of Inbe (a private house attached to a shrine). Relatedly, in the ``Shinshokakuchofusho'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), there are mentions of 10 of the 20 households of Inabe deity in Kobe . It is certain that Kobe was located at Naru-jinja Shrine from the description of ``50 bunches of sake rice, divine tax'' in the Shijiki-sai Ainosai article of the ``Engishiki'', so it is thought that these Inabe-Kobe were attached to Naru-jinja Shrine, and Naru-jinja Shrine was enshrined as the guardian deity of Kii Inabe. It is also assumed that these Kobes were distributed around Narukami, Wakayama City, where Narukami Shrine is located .In addition, in "{{jalink|Kogo shui|Q531555}}", Kiibe is a descendant of {{jalink|Hikosato no Mikoto|Q135327097}}, and is the ruler of Nagusa District. It is written that he lived in Kigo and Arakago (neither of which have "Wamyosho") and was responsible for paying tribute of lumber and building palaces and shrines. . === Outline === In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} from the 8th grade of Jugoi to the 8th grade of Jushii (Junior 4th rank and lower rank) in the first year ({{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nagusa District|Q11415547}} has "Narujin Shrine Meishin University It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Ainame Shinnai'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Ainamesai|Q11581944}}・It is written that it was deposited in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} at {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Narukinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. There are many famous shrines in Kii Province, but the ones that received dedications at the three festivals are Narukinja Shrine, {{jalink|Maezen Shrine, Kunikake Shrine|Q888184|Maejin Shrine}}, {{ja There were only four shrines: link|Mae-mae Jingu/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|Kunikake Shrine}} and {{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}}. Regarding these four companies, please refer to "{{jalink|Reishūka|Q11378321}}" Jingirei"Reishūkai" Volume 7 (Jingirei) Nakafuyujo Kamiusaōsai. Also, there is a description that it was listed at Hoheisha Shrine on the occasion of the Aijo Festival on Kami U no Hi in November as ``Mae, Kokukensu, Itaukeso, Narukami'' {{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In the storage rice book of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}3 ({{jalink|1048|Q19359}}), 340 steps of Narukami Shrine rice fields can be seen {{Sfn|Narukin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'', it is written as ``Shoichii Naru-Okami'' {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 13 ({{jalink|1585|Q6736}}), {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}'s {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}} {{jalink|Ota Castle (Ki Province)|Q10941625|Ota Castle}}It is said that during the water attack, the surrounding area, including Narukin Shrine, was greatly devastated. The shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), and five Kanda shrines were donated {{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. In the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines, the nearby Shikinai shrines Kenmaon Shrine and Katsuchi Shrine were enshrined within the shrine grounds. === God level === * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} January 27, 1985 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - The notation is "Narukami". * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{jalink|Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}'') - Written as "Narutokami". === Precincts === The main hall consists of two halls. {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} is enshrined in the right shrine (facing the left shrine), and {{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko/Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto/Hayaakitsuhime-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined in the left shrine. Katsuchi Shrine: Shikinai-sha (small), former village-sha |Soken = |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} (Both shrines) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} |Shinto ritual = }} === Kenmaon Shrine === ``Katamaoto Shrine'' (Katamanoto Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukin Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kotsuchi Shrine. The following deities are enshrined: * Kamiadaka Ashitsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} *: Another name for {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}, he was conferred {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}}, which is the upper rank of Jugoi, and in the seventh year of Jogan ({{jalink|865 In 1983|Q30350}}), it was ranked as a government shrine, and in the 8th year of Jogan ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), there is a record that it was conferred the rank of Junior Fourth Rank and Lower.{{Sfn|Kanshin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}."{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink |Nagusa District|Q11415547}} is described as "Kanmaon Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, and in "{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}" it is listed as "Shoichii Shrine". ``Arimaon Okami'' is compared to Kenmaon Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Relatedly, in "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", "Arima" is mentioned as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District, and it is determined that it is near Narukami, Wakayama City. . Kenmaon Shrine was originally located approximately 300 meters northeast of Naru Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsune Shrine Kyoho Kotatsu'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}) {{Sfn|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} April 7th ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q71253 4}}, {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} listed as {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. Later, a branch spirit was summoned to the shrine in Wakayama City, and the shrine building was rebuilt (currently Kenma-on Shrine, {{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Kanma-on Shrine (descendant shrine)}}). The shrine is as follows. * Records of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms (all written as "Kanma Ongami") ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} May 26th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to upper junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') ** On May 17, 7th year of Jogan era ({{jalink|865 years|Q30350}}), he was listed as a government shrine (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') ** Leap March 13, 8th year of the Jogan era ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii ("{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}") - Written as "Arimaon Okami". === Katsuchi Shrine === '''Katsuchi Shrine'' (Katsuchi Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukinja Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kenmaon Shrine. The following deities are enshrined. * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Encounter with Tsuchi no Mikoto}} (Kagutsuchi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sakamatsu Shrine (Narukami, Wakayama City)
The former enshrinement site of Katsuchi Shrine. }}]] "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11 415547}}, it is written as ``Katsuchi Shrine'' and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, but in some manuscripts it is annotated as ``Narukami Sue Shrine'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Katsuchijin'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Kotsuchi Shrine was originally located about 200 meters northeast of Naruko Shrine. rd|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Gakamatsu Shrine (formerly Katsuchi Shrine)}}). {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}} It is said that it was in ruins in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} due to the military fire of the {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsuchijin Keian Kotora'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Keian|Q1146135}}3 ({{jalink|1650|Q6951}}) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) "Nanki Jinja Roku" describes Kotsuchi Shrine as "Muhoden erected stone monument" {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In April of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern company ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. The shrine is as follows. * After Six Kingdoms History** Junior Fourth Rank Upper (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Katsuchijin''. === Others === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Narutake Shrine}}]] '''Precinct shrine''' * Amaterasu Daijingu ShrineThe description of other shrines is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narukin Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Kasugasha * Yume Shrine * Inarisha ``Kingaisha'''' * Narutake Shrine *: Another name is "Tsubo Gozen" . It is enshrined in front of the torii gate of Naru Shrine. The enshrined deity, Narumu Daimyojin, is said to be the fourth princess of King Kija of {{jalink|Baekje|Q28428}}, and is said to have come from Baekje to Kii Province around the 5th century and introduced sake brewing. Currently, only the stone shrine remains, but in ancient times there was a shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}1089 '''Access''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway|Q11417705}} (Wakayama Electric Railway) {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway Kishigawa Line|Q6416586|Kishigawa Line}} {{jalink|Naemiya Station|Q11504968}} (about 12 minutes walk) * {{jalink|Wakayama Bus|Q11417290}} {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|Wakayama Station|Q1044107}} From the east exit, Narukami Line 90/94 Narukami Jutaku-mae (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} * Narukami Shell Midden -
