{{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Foundation = (legendary) 6th {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Flowered construction|Q210684}}2 buildings |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Narujinja|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Narukami Shrine'' (Narujinja) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhiko Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhime Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}}''' (Amenofutodama no Mikoto) - Right shrine (facing left shrine). {{jalink|Ibe-san|Q8189229}}Ancestor God. The two deities enshrined are the gods of Mito (the gods of the port). As far back as the {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}), in the "Nanki Shrine Record", it appears that the two deities, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, are enshrined {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. However, the earlier "Nanki Meishoshi" states that it is unknown, and it is thought that the enshrined deity was unknown in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}{{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Later, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}},'' he questioned the theory that two gods were enshrined, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, since there was only one deity enshrined at Narukin Shrine in the ``Engishiki,'' and advocated the theory that Tentadama-no-Mikoto was the enshrined deity.{{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. This speculation is based on the theory that the area around this area is compared to the area of ``Ibe-go'' that appears in ``{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}'', and in the same text, it is said that a person lived in this area. {jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kiibe clan) is said to have enshrined its ancestral deity, Tenta Tama no Mikoto {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In addition, the same article states that when Naru Shrine, which had been in ruins, was rebuilt in 4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), two deities were considered: Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime. However, Kii-Ibe is not a family of Inebekushi whose ancestor is Tenta Tama-no-Mikoto, but a family of {{jalink|Kikokuzō}}, and {{jalink|Hikosa Chi-no-Mikoto|Q135327097}} is its ancestor deity, according to ``{{jalink|Kogo Shui|Q531555}}.'' Also, the fourth book of ``Mochizuki'' and ``Sho Keiki,'' which is another name for the founder of Kikuni-zukuri, {{jalink|Takuma Tama no Mikoto}}. 's father is said to be a Narukami ({{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}) called {{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}, and in the Otomo clan genealogy in "Furuya family family history" and "Kamibetsu genealogy," Tenrai no Mikoto is said to be ``Narukami Shrine (Koreya), Nagusa district, Kii province.''Keninobu Nakata "Shinbetsu Genealogy", {{jalink|Tokyo National Museum|Q653433}} Digital Library. .Based on the Tentaitama no Mikoto theory, in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}), {{jalink|Tentadama no Mikoto Shrine|Q11] is the stronghold of the Inabe clan. 442508}} ({{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}Ibe Town), the spirit of Tentadama no Mikoto was once again solicited and enshrined in the main shrine.. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine biography cited in "Ki Meisho Zue", around the time of {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} (sixth generation), the god {{jalink|Ha It is said that there was an oracle to enshrine Yaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and Naru-jinja Shrine was founded as a result of this enshrinement. The company name ``Naru'' is said to be a name related to a river, as it is assumed in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'' that it means water flowing from a valley called ``Narumizu'' or ``Narutaki''. {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Gods)|1986}}On the other hand, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) link|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", you can see the entry "Inbe" as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District.. This is believed to be near Inbe, Wakayama City, south of Narukin Shrine, and the area is said to have been the {{jalink|honkan|Q846706|honkanchi}} of {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kii Inbe) in Kii. Nagusa District, Ibe-go section, Kobe-go section. . Also, in the same book, ``Nabe'' appears next to ``Ibe'', but this is a misprint of ``Kobe'', and is thought to mean ``Ibe Kobe'', that is, the god of Inbe (a private house attached to a shrine). Relatedly, in the ``Shinshokakuchofusho'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), there are mentions of 10 of the 20 households of Inabe deity in Kobe . It is certain that Kobe was located at Naru-jinja Shrine from the description of ``50 bunches of sake rice, divine tax'' in the Shijiki-sai Ainosai article of the ``Engishiki'', so it is thought that these Inabe-Kobe were attached to Naru-jinja Shrine, and Naru-jinja Shrine was enshrined as the guardian deity of Kii Inabe. It is also assumed that these Kobes were distributed around Narukami, Wakayama City, where Narukami Shrine is located .In addition, in "{{jalink|Kogo shui|Q531555}}", Kiibe is a descendant of {{jalink|Hikosato no Mikoto|Q135327097}}, and is the ruler of Nagusa District. It is written that he lived in Kigo and Arakago (neither of which have "Wamyosho") and was responsible for paying tribute of lumber and building palaces and shrines. . === Outline === In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} from the 8th grade of Jugoi to the 8th grade of Jushii (Junior 4th rank and lower rank) in the first year ({{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nagusa District|Q11415547}} has "Narujin Shrine Meishin University It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Ainame Shinnai'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Ainamesai|Q11581944}}・It is written that it was deposited in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} at {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Narukinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. There are many famous shrines in Kii Province, but the ones that received dedications at the three festivals are Narukinja Shrine, {{jalink|Maezen Shrine, Kunikake Shrine|Q888184|Maejin Shrine}}, {{ja There were only four shrines: link|Mae-mae Jingu/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|Kunikake Shrine}} and {{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}}. Regarding these four companies, please refer to "{{jalink|Reishūka|Q11378321}}" Jingirei"Reishūkai" Volume 7 (Jingirei) Nakafuyujo Kamiusaōsai. Also, there is a description that it was listed at Hoheisha Shrine on the occasion of the Aijo Festival on Kami U no Hi in November as ``Mae, Kokukensu, Itaukeso, Narukami'' {{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In the storage rice book of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}3 ({{jalink|1048|Q19359}}), 340 steps of Narukami Shrine rice fields can be seen {{Sfn|Narukin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'', it is written as ``Shoichii Naru-Okami'' {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 13 ({{jalink|1585|Q6736}}), {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}'s {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}} {{jalink|Ota Castle (Ki Province)|Q10941625|Ota Castle}}It is said that during the water attack, the surrounding area, including Narukin Shrine, was greatly devastated. The shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), and five Kanda shrines were donated {{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. In the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines, the nearby Shikinai shrines Kenmaon Shrine and Katsuchi Shrine were enshrined within the shrine grounds. === God level === * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} January 27, 1985 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - The notation is "Narukami". * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{jalink|Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}'') - Written as "Narutokami". === Precincts === The main hall consists of two halls. {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} is enshrined in the right shrine (facing the left shrine), and {{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko/Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto/Hayaakitsuhime-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined in the left shrine. File:Naru-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Naru-jinja haiden.JPG|House of worship === Setsusha === {{Shrine |Name = Kenmaon Shrine/Katsuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Naru-jinja Katamaoto & Katsuchi-jinja.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = |Position = |Enshrined deity = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Konohananosakuyabime|Q1781862|Kamigota Shikaashitsuhime no Mikoto}}
Katsuchi Shrine: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kibutsuchi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} (small), former {{jalink|mura-sha|Q134917284}}
Katsuchi Shrine: Shikinai-sha (small), former village-sha |Soken = |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} (Both shrines) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} |Shinto ritual = }} === Kenmaon Shrine === ``Katamaoto Shrine'' (Katamanoto Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukin Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kotsuchi Shrine. The following deities are enshrined: * Kamiadaka Ashitsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} *: Another name for {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}, he was conferred {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}}, which is the upper rank of Jugoi, and in the seventh year of Jogan ({{jalink|865 In 1983|Q30350}}), it was ranked as a government shrine, and in the 8th year of Jogan ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), there is a record that it was conferred the rank of Junior Fourth Rank and Lower.{{Sfn|Kanshin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}."{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink |Nagusa District|Q11415547}} is described as "Kanmaon Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, and in "{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}" it is listed as "Shoichii Shrine". ``Arimaon Okami'' is compared to Kenmaon Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Relatedly, in "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", "Arima" is mentioned as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District, and it is determined that it is near Narukami, Wakayama City. . Kenmaon Shrine was originally located approximately 300 meters northeast of Naru Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsune Shrine Kyoho Kotatsu'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}) {{Sfn|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} April 7th ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q71253 4}}, {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} listed as {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. Later, a branch spirit was summoned to the shrine in Wakayama City, and the shrine building was rebuilt (currently Kenma-on Shrine, {{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Kanma-on Shrine (descendant shrine)}}). The shrine is as follows. * Records of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms (all written as "Kanma Ongami") ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} May 26th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to upper junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') ** On May 17, 7th year of Jogan era ({{jalink|865 years|Q30350}}), he was listed as a government shrine (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') ** Leap March 13, 8th year of the Jogan era ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii ("{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}") - Written as "Arimaon Okami". === Katsuchi Shrine === '''Katsuchi Shrine'' (Katsuchi Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukinja Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kenmaon Shrine. The following deities are enshrined. * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Encounter with Tsuchi no Mikoto}} (Kagutsuchi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sakamatsu Shrine (Narukami, Wakayama City)
The former enshrinement site of Katsuchi Shrine. }}]] "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11 415547}}, it is written as ``Katsuchi Shrine'' and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, but in some manuscripts it is annotated as ``Narukami Sue Shrine'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Katsuchijin'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Kotsuchi Shrine was originally located about 200 meters northeast of Naruko Shrine. rd|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Gakamatsu Shrine (formerly Katsuchi Shrine)}}). {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}} It is said that it was in ruins in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} due to the military fire of the {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsuchijin Keian Kotora'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Keian|Q1146135}}3 ({{jalink|1650|Q6951}}) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) "Nanki Jinja Roku" describes Kotsuchi Shrine as "Muhoden erected stone monument" {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In April of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern company ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. The shrine is as follows. * After Six Kingdoms History** Junior Fourth Rank Upper (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Katsuchijin''. === Others === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Narutake Shrine}}]] '''Precinct shrine''' * Amaterasu Daijingu ShrineThe description of other shrines is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narukin Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Kasugasha * Yume Shrine * Inarisha ``Kingaisha'''' * Narutake Shrine *: Another name is "Tsubo Gozen" . It is enshrined in front of the torii gate of Naru Shrine. The enshrined deity, Narumu Daimyojin, is said to be the fourth princess of King Kija of {{jalink|Baekje|Q28428}}, and is said to have come from Baekje to Kii Province around the 5th century and introduced sake brewing. Currently, only the stone shrine remains, but in ancient times there was a shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}1089 '''Access''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway|Q11417705}} (Wakayama Electric Railway) {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway Kishigawa Line|Q6416586|Kishigawa Line}} {{jalink|Naemiya Station|Q11504968}} (about 12 minutes walk) * {{jalink|Wakayama Bus|Q11417290}} {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|Wakayama Station|Q1044107}} From the east exit, Narukami Line 90/94 Narukami Jutaku-mae (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} * Narukami Shell Midden - {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Company status = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|Meishin University}})
Old {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}} |Foundation = (legendary) 6th {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} |Main shrine = {{jalink|Flowered construction|Q210684}}2 buildings |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Narujinja|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Narukami Shrine'' (Narujinja) is located in {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}. {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}}) and {{jalink|Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|Old shrine rank}} is {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhiko Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhime Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}}''' (Amenofutodama no Mikoto) - Right shrine (facing left shrine). {{jalink|Ibe-san|Q8189229}}Ancestor God. The two deities enshrined are the gods of Mito (the gods of the port). As far back as the {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}), in the "Nanki Shrine Record", it appears that the two deities, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, are enshrined {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. However, the earlier "Nanki Meishoshi" states that it is unknown, and it is thought that the enshrined deity was unknown in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}}{{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Later, in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}},'' he questioned the theory that two gods were enshrined, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, since there was only one deity enshrined at Narukin Shrine in the ``Engishiki,'' and advocated the theory that Tentadama-no-Mikoto was the enshrined deity.{{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. This speculation is based on the theory that the area around this area is compared to the area of ``Ibe-go'' that appears in ``{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}'', and in the same text, it is said that a person lived in this area. {jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kiibe clan) is said to have enshrined its ancestral deity, Tenta Tama no Mikoto {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In addition, the same article states that when Naru Shrine, which had been in ruins, was rebuilt in 4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), two deities were considered: Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime. However, Kii-Ibe is not a family of Inebekushi whose ancestor is Tenta Tama-no-Mikoto, but a family of {{jalink|Kikokuzō}}, and {{jalink|Hikosa Chi-no-Mikoto|Q135327097}} is its ancestor deity, according to ``{{jalink|Kogo Shui|Q531555}}.'' Also, the fourth book of ``Mochizuki'' and ``Sho Keiki,'' which is another name for the founder of Kikuni-zukuri, {{jalink|Takuma Tama no Mikoto}}. 's father is said to be a Narukami ({{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}) called {{jalink|Raijin|Q12808082}}, and in the Otomo clan genealogy in "Furuya family family history" and "Kamibetsu genealogy," Tenrai no Mikoto is said to be ``Narukami Shrine (Koreya), Nagusa district, Kii province.''Keninobu Nakata "Shinbetsu Genealogy", {{jalink|Tokyo National Museum|Q653433}} Digital Library. .Based on the Tentaitama no Mikoto theory, in {{jalink|Heisei|Q234510}}8 ({{jalink|1996|Q2070}}), {{jalink|Tentadama no Mikoto Shrine|Q11] is the stronghold of the Inabe clan. 442508}} ({{jalink|Nara Prefecture|Q131287}}{{jalink|Kashihara City|Q496319}}Ibe Town), the spirit of Tentadama no Mikoto was once again solicited and enshrined in the main shrine.. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine biography cited in "Ki Meisho Zue", around the time of {{jalink|Emperor Koan|Q312821}} (sixth generation), the god {{jalink|Ha It is said that there was an oracle to enshrine Yaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and Naru-jinja Shrine was founded as a result of this enshrinement. The company name ``Naru'' is said to be a name related to a river, as it is assumed in ``{{jalink|Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964}}'' that it means water flowing from a valley called ``Narumizu'' or ``Narutaki''. {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Gods)|1986}}On the other hand, {{jalink|Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|Shohei}} ({{jalink|931|Q24789}}-{{jalink|938|Q24794}}) link|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", you can see the entry "Inbe" as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District.. This is believed to be near Inbe, Wakayama City, south of Narukin Shrine, and the area is said to have been the {{jalink|honkan|Q846706|honkanchi}} of {{jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kii Inbe) in Kii. Nagusa District, Ibe-go section, Kobe-go section. . Also, in the same book, ``Nabe'' appears next to ``Ibe'', but this is a misprint of ``Kobe'', and is thought to mean ``Ibe Kobe'', that is, the god of Inbe (a private house attached to a shrine). Relatedly, in the ``Shinshokakuchofusho'' {{jalink|Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|Daido}} 1st year ({{jalink|806|Q30021}}), there are mentions of 10 of the 20 households of Inabe deity in Kobe . It is certain that Kobe was located at Naru-jinja Shrine from the description of ``50 bunches of sake rice, divine tax'' in the Shijiki-sai Ainosai article of the ``Engishiki'', so it is thought that these Inabe-Kobe were attached to Naru-jinja Shrine, and Naru-jinja Shrine was enshrined as the guardian deity of Kii Inabe. It is also assumed that these Kobes were distributed around Narukami, Wakayama City, where Narukami Shrine is located .In addition, in "{{jalink|Kogo shui|Q531555}}", Kiibe is a descendant of {{jalink|Hikosato no Mikoto|Q135327097}}, and is the ruler of Nagusa District. It is written that he lived in Kigo and Arakago (neither of which have "Wamyosho") and was responsible for paying tribute of lumber and building palaces and shrines. . === Outline === In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), there is an article about the promotion of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} from the 8th grade of Jugoi to the 8th grade of Jushii (Junior 4th rank and lower rank) in the first year ({{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}}, {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nagusa District|Q11415547}} has "Narujin Shrine Meishin University It will be listed at {{jalink|Meishin Taisha|Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Ainame Shinnai'' and {{jalink|Monthly Festival|Q11516161}} and {{jalink|Ainamesai|Q11581944}}・It is written that it was deposited in {{jalink|Heihaku|Q11481885}} at {{jalink|Ninamesai|Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Narukinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. There are many famous shrines in Kii Province, but the ones that received dedications at the three festivals are Narukinja Shrine, {{jalink|Maezen Shrine, Kunikake Shrine|Q888184|Maejin Shrine}}, {{ja There were only four shrines: link|Mae-mae Jingu/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|Kunikake Shrine}} and {{jalink|Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271}}. Regarding these four companies, please refer to "{{jalink|Reishūka|Q11378321}}" Jingirei"Reishūkai" Volume 7 (Jingirei) Nakafuyujo Kamiusaōsai. Also, there is a description that it was listed at Hoheisha Shrine on the occasion of the Aijo Festival on Kami U no Hi in November as ``Mae, Kokukensu, Itaukeso, Narukami'' {{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In the storage rice book of {{jalink|Eiroku|Q1143341}}3 ({{jalink|1048|Q19359}}), 340 steps of Narukami Shrine rice fields can be seen {{Sfn|Narukin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'', it is written as ``Shoichii Naru-Okami'' {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Tensho|Q1145058}} In 13 ({{jalink|1585|Q6736}}), {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550}}'s {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}} {{jalink|Ota Castle (Ki Province)|Q10941625|Ota Castle}}It is said that during the water attack, the surrounding area, including Narukin Shrine, was greatly devastated. The shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}4 ({{jalink|1719|Q6902}}), and five Kanda shrines were donated {{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system|Q712534}}. In the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines, the nearby Shikinai shrines Kenmaon Shrine and Katsuchi Shrine were enshrined within the shrine grounds. === God level === * Records of {{jalink|Divine rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} January 27, 1985 ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}") - The notation is "Narukami". * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{jalink|Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}'') - Written as "Narutokami". === Precincts === The main hall consists of two halls. {{jalink|Futodama|Q11059539|Tentadama no Mikoto}} is enshrined in the right shrine (facing the left shrine), and {{jalink|Hayaakitsuhiko/Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto/Hayaakitsuhime-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined in the left shrine. File:Naru-jinja honden.JPG|Main shrine File:Naru-jinja haiden.JPG|House of worship === Setsusha === {{Shrine |Name = Kenmaon Shrine/Katsuchi Shrine |Image = [[File:Naru-jinja Katamaoto & Katsuchi-jinja.JPG|280px]]
Shrine building |Location = |Position = |Enshrined deity = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Konohananosakuyabime|Q1781862|Kamigota Shikaashitsuhime no Mikoto}}
Katsuchi Shrine: {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Kibutsuchi no Mikoto}} |Divinity = |Shaku = Kenmaon Shrine: {{jalink|Shikiuchi-sha}} (small), former {{jalink|mura-sha|Q134917284}}
Katsuchi Shrine: Shikinai-sha (small), former village-sha |Soken = |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{jalink|October 12th|Q2920}} (Both shrines) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} |Shinto ritual = }} === Kenmaon Shrine === ``Katamaoto Shrine'' (Katamanoto Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukin Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kotsuchi Shrine. The following deities are enshrined: * Kamiadaka Ashitsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} *: Another name for {{jalink|Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. In national history, {{jalink|Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} In the first year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}, he was conferred {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}}, which is the upper rank of Jugoi, and in the seventh year of Jogan ({{jalink|865 In 1983|Q30350}}), it was ranked as a government shrine, and in the 8th year of Jogan ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), there is a record that it was conferred the rank of Junior Fourth Rank and Lower.{{Sfn|Kanshin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}."{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink |Nagusa District|Q11415547}} is described as "Kanmaon Shrine" and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, and in "{{jalink|Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}" it is listed as "Shoichii Shrine". ``Arimaon Okami'' is compared to Kenmaon Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Relatedly, in "{{jalink|Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", "Arima" is mentioned as {{jalink|go|Q130458414}} of Nagusa District, and it is determined that it is near Narukami, Wakayama City. . Kenmaon Shrine was originally located approximately 300 meters northeast of Naru Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsune Shrine Kyoho Kotatsu'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Kyoho|Q1145048}}9 ({{jalink|1724|Q6926}}) {{Sfn|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.{{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} April 7th ({{jalink|1874|Q7761}}), {{jalink|Modern company rating system|Q71253 4}}, {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} listed as {{jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. Later, a branch spirit was summoned to the shrine in Wakayama City, and the shrine building was rebuilt (currently Kenma-on Shrine, {{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Kanma-on Shrine (descendant shrine)}}). The shrine is as follows. * Records of {{jalink|Divine Rank|Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms (all written as "Kanma Ongami") ** {{jalink|Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|Jōgan}} May 26th, 1st year ({{jalink|859|Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to upper junior fifth rank (``{{jalink|Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981}}'') ** On May 17, 7th year of Jogan era ({{jalink|865 years|Q30350}}), he was listed as a government shrine (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') ** Leap March 13, 8th year of the Jogan era ({{jalink|866|Q23872}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii ("{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008}}") - Written as "Arimaon Okami". === Katsuchi Shrine === '''Katsuchi Shrine'' (Katsuchi Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukinja Shrine. {{jalink|Modern company rank system|Q712534|Old company rank}} is {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kenmaon Shrine. The following deities are enshrined. * {{jalink|Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|Encounter with Tsuchi no Mikoto}} (Kagutsuchi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sakamatsu Shrine (Narukami, Wakayama City)
The former enshrinement site of Katsuchi Shrine. }}]] "{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}" {{jalink|Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|Shinmyocho}} is {{jalink|Kiokuni|Q864053}}{{jalink|Nakusa District|Q11 415547}}, it is written as ``Katsuchi Shrine'' and is listed as {{jalink|Shikiuchi}}, but in some manuscripts it is annotated as ``Narukami Sue Shrine'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{jalink|Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008}}'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Katsuchijin'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Kotsuchi Shrine was originally located about 200 meters northeast of Naruko Shrine. rd|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Gakamatsu Shrine (formerly Katsuchi Shrine)}}). {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}} It is said that it was in ruins in the early {{jalink|Edo period|Q184963}} due to the military fire of the {{jalink|Kishū Conquest|Q10855721}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsuchijin Keian Kotora'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{jalink|Keian|Q1146135}}3 ({{jalink|1650|Q6951}}) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{jalink|Enkyo|Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{jalink|1746|Q7589}}) "Nanki Jinja Roku" describes Kotsuchi Shrine as "Muhoden erected stone monument" {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In April of {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}}6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), he was ranked as {{jalink|murasha|Q134917284}} in the {{jalink|modern company ranking system|Q712534}}. In 1907 ({{jalink|1907|Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. The shrine is as follows. * After Six Kingdoms History** Junior Fourth Rank Upper (``{{jalink|Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Katsuchijin''. === Others === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Narutake Shrine}}]] '''Precinct shrine''' * Amaterasu Daijingu ShrineThe description of other shrines is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narukin Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Kasugasha * Yume Shrine * Inarisha ``Kingaisha'''' * Narutake Shrine *: Another name is "Tsubo Gozen" . It is enshrined in front of the torii gate of Naru Shrine. The enshrined deity, Narumu Daimyojin, is said to be the fourth princess of King Kija of {{jalink|Baekje|Q28428}}, and is said to have come from Baekje to Kii Province around the 5th century and introduced sake brewing. Currently, only the stone shrine remains, but in ancient times there was a shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314}}{{jalink|Wakayama City|Q200747}}{{jalink|Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|Narukami}}1089 '''Access''' * {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway|Q11417705}} (Wakayama Electric Railway) {{jalink|Wakayama Electric Railway Kishigawa Line|Q6416586|Kishigawa Line}} {{jalink|Naemiya Station|Q11504968}} (about 12 minutes walk) * {{jalink|Wakayama Bus|Q11417290}} {{jalink|West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|JR}}{{jalink|Wakayama Station|Q1044107}} From the east exit, Narukami Line 90/94 Narukami Jutaku-mae (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * {{jalink|Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184}} * Narukami Shell Midden - Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Foundation = (legendary) 6th {{ill|lt=Emperor Koan|Q312821|WD=Q312821}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Flowered construction|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}2 buildings |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 12th|Q2920|WD=Q2920}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Narujinja|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Narukami Shrine'' (Narujinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}{{ill|lt=Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|WD=Q17209155|lt=Narukami}}. {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * '''{{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhiko Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhime Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Tentadama no Mikoto}}''' (Amenofutodama no Mikoto) - Right shrine (facing left shrine). {{ill|lt=Ibe-san|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}Ancestor God. The two deities enshrined are the gods of Mito (the gods of the port). As far back as the {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}), in the "Nanki Shrine Record", it appears that the two deities, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, are enshrined {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. However, the earlier "Nanki Meishoshi" states that it is unknown, and it is thought that the enshrined deity was unknown in the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}{{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Later, in ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}},'' he questioned the theory that two gods were enshrined, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, since there was only one deity enshrined at Narukin Shrine in the ``Engishiki,'' and advocated the theory that Tentadama-no-Mikoto was the enshrined deity.{{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. This speculation is based on the theory that the area around this area is compared to the area of ``Ibe-go'' that appears in ``{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}'', and in the same text, it is said that a person lived in this area. {jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kiibe clan) is said to have enshrined its ancestral deity, Tenta Tama no Mikoto {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In addition, the same article states that when Naru Shrine, which had been in ruins, was rebuilt in 4 ({{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902}}), two deities were considered: Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime. However, Kii-Ibe is not a family of Inebekushi whose ancestor is Tenta Tama-no-Mikoto, but a family of {{ill||lt=Kikokuzō|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Hikosa Chi-no-Mikoto|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}} is its ancestor deity, according to ``{{ill|lt=Kogo Shui|Q531555|WD=Q531555}}.'' Also, the fourth book of ``Mochizuki'' and ``Sho Keiki,'' which is another name for the founder of Kikuni-zukuri, {{ill||lt=Takuma Tama no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. 's father is said to be a Narukami ({{ill|lt=Raijin|Q12808082|WD=Q12808082}}) called {{ill|lt=Raijin|Q12808082|WD=Q12808082}}, and in the Otomo clan genealogy in "Furuya family family history" and "Kamibetsu genealogy," Tenrai no Mikoto is said to be ``Narukami Shrine (Koreya), Nagusa district, Kii province.''Keninobu Nakata "Shinbetsu Genealogy", {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433}} Digital Library. .Based on the Tentaitama no Mikoto theory, in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}), {{ill||lt=Tentadama no Mikoto Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}Ibe Town), the spirit of Tentadama no Mikoto was once again solicited and enshrined in the main shrine.. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine biography cited in "Ki Meisho Zue", around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Koan|Q312821|WD=Q312821}} (sixth generation), the god {{ill|lt=Ha It is said that there was an oracle to enshrine Yaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and Naru-jinja Shrine was founded as a result of this enshrinement. The company name ``Naru'' is said to be a name related to a river, as it is assumed in ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}'' that it means water flowing from a valley called ``Narumizu'' or ``Narutaki''. {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Gods)|1986}}On the other hand, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) link|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", you can see the entry "Inbe" as {{ill|lt=go|Q130458414|WD=Q130458414}} of Nagusa District.. This is believed to be near Inbe, Wakayama City, south of Narukin Shrine, and the area is said to have been the {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706|lt=honkanchi}} of {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}} (Kii Inbe) in Kii. Nagusa District, Ibe-go section, Kobe-go section. . Also, in the same book, ``Nabe'' appears next to ``Ibe'', but this is a misprint of ``Kobe'', and is thought to mean ``Ibe Kobe'', that is, the god of Inbe (a private house attached to a shrine). Relatedly, in the ``Shinshokakuchofusho'' {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), there are mentions of 10 of the 20 households of Inabe deity in Kobe . It is certain that Kobe was located at Naru-jinja Shrine from the description of ``50 bunches of sake rice, divine tax'' in the Shijiki-sai Ainosai article of the ``Engishiki'', so it is thought that these Inabe-Kobe were attached to Naru-jinja Shrine, and Naru-jinja Shrine was enshrined as the guardian deity of Kii Inabe. It is also assumed that these Kobes were distributed around Narukami, Wakayama City, where Narukami Shrine is located .In addition, in "{{ill|lt=Kogo shui|Q531555|WD=Q531555}}", Kiibe is a descendant of {{ill|lt=Hikosato no Mikoto|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}, and is the ruler of Nagusa District. It is written that he lived in Kigo and Arakago (neither of which have "Wamyosho") and was responsible for paying tribute of lumber and building palaces and shrines. . === Outline === In national history, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), there is an article about the promotion of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} from the 8th grade of Jugoi to the 8th grade of Jushii (Junior 4th rank and lower rank) in the first year ({{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} has "Narujin Shrine Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Ainame Shinnai'' and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|lt=Ainamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}・It is written that it was deposited in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} at {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Narukinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. There are many famous shrines in Kii Province, but the ones that received dedications at the three festivals are Narukinja Shrine, {{ill|lt=Maezen Shrine, Kunikake Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184|lt=Maejin Shrine}}, {{ja There were only four shrines: link|Mae-mae Jingu/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|Kunikake Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}}. Regarding these four companies, please refer to "{{ill|lt=Reishūka|Q11378321|WD=Q11378321}}" Jingirei"Reishūkai" Volume 7 (Jingirei) Nakafuyujo Kamiusaōsai. Also, there is a description that it was listed at Hoheisha Shrine on the occasion of the Aijo Festival on Kami U no Hi in November as ``Mae, Kokukensu, Itaukeso, Narukami'' {{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In the storage rice book of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}}3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359}}), 340 steps of Narukami Shrine rice fields can be seen {{Sfn|Narukin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', it is written as ``Shoichii Naru-Okami'' {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} In 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}), {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}'s {{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721}} {{ill|lt=Ota Castle (Ki Province)|Q10941625|WD=Q10941625|lt=Ota Castle}}It is said that during the water attack, the surrounding area, including Narukin Shrine, was greatly devastated. The shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}4 ({{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902}}), and five Kanda shrines were donated {{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines, the nearby Shikinai shrines Kenmaon Shrine and Katsuchi Shrine were enshrined within the shrine grounds. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} January 27, 1985 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}") - The notation is "Narukami". * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as "Narutokami". === Precincts === The main hall consists of two halls. {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Tentadama no Mikoto}} is enshrined in the right shrine (facing the left shrine), and {{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko/Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto/Hayaakitsuhime-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined in the left shrine. Katsuchi Shrine: Shikinai-sha (small), former village-sha |Soken = |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 12th|Q2920|WD=Q2920}} (Both shrines) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} |Shinto ritual = }} === Kenmaon Shrine === ``Katamaoto Shrine'' (Katamanoto Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukin Shrine. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kotsuchi Shrine. The following deities are enshrined: * Kamiadaka Ashitsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} *: Another name for {{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. In national history, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}, he was conferred {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is the upper rank of Jugoi, and in the seventh year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865 In 1983|Q30350|WD=Q30350}}), it was ranked as a government shrine, and in the 8th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), there is a record that it was conferred the rank of Junior Fourth Rank and Lower.{{Sfn|Kanshin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}."{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} is {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{jalink |Nagusa District|Q11415547}} is described as "Kanmaon Shrine" and is listed as {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}}, and in "{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}" it is listed as "Shoichii Shrine". ``Arimaon Okami'' is compared to Kenmaon Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Relatedly, in "{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}", "Arima" is mentioned as {{ill|lt=go|Q130458414|WD=Q130458414}} of Nagusa District, and it is determined that it is near Narukami, Wakayama City. . Kenmaon Shrine was originally located approximately 300 meters northeast of Naru Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsune Shrine Kyoho Kotatsu'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}9 ({{ill|lt=1724|Q6926|WD=Q6926}}) {{Sfn|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} April 7th ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}), {{ill||lt=Modern company rating system|UNKNOWN}}, {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} listed as {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In 1907 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. Later, a branch spirit was summoned to the shrine in Wakayama City, and the shrine building was rebuilt (currently Kenma-on Shrine, {{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Kanma-on Shrine (descendant shrine)}}). The shrine is as follows. * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms (all written as "Kanma Ongami") ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} May 26th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to upper junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') ** On May 17, 7th year of Jogan era ({{ill|lt=865 years|Q30350|WD=Q30350}}), he was listed as a government shrine (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') ** Leap March 13, 8th year of the Jogan era ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii ("{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}") - Written as "Arimaon Okami". === Katsuchi Shrine === '''Katsuchi Shrine'' (Katsuchi Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukinja Shrine. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kenmaon Shrine. The following deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Encounter with Tsuchi no Mikoto}} (Kagutsuchi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sakamatsu Shrine (Narukami, Wakayama City)
The former enshrinement site of Katsuchi Shrine. }}]] "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} is {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill||lt=Nakusa District|UNKNOWN}}, it is written as ``Katsuchi Shrine'' and is listed as {{ill||lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}}, but in some manuscripts it is annotated as ``Narukami Sue Shrine'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Katsuchijin'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Kotsuchi Shrine was originally located about 200 meters northeast of Naruko Shrine. rd|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Gakamatsu Shrine (formerly Katsuchi Shrine)}}). {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}} It is said that it was in ruins in the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} due to the military fire of the {{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsuchijin Keian Kotora'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{ill|lt=Keian|Q1146135|WD=Q1146135}}3 ({{ill|lt=1650|Q6951|WD=Q6951}}) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) "Nanki Jinja Roku" describes Kotsuchi Shrine as "Muhoden erected stone monument" {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In April of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), he was ranked as {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the {{ill|lt=modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In 1907 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. The shrine is as follows. * After Six Kingdoms History** Junior Fourth Rank Upper (``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Katsuchijin''. === Others === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Narutake Shrine}}]] '''Precinct shrine''' * Amaterasu Daijingu ShrineThe description of other shrines is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narukin Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Kasugasha * Yume Shrine * Inarisha ``Kingaisha'''' * Narutake Shrine *: Another name is "Tsubo Gozen" . It is enshrined in front of the torii gate of Naru Shrine. The enshrined deity, Narumu Daimyojin, is said to be the fourth princess of King Kija of {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428}}, and is said to have come from Baekje to Kii Province around the 5th century and introduced sake brewing. Currently, only the stone shrine remains, but in ancient times there was a shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}{{ill|lt=Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|WD=Q17209155|lt=Narukami}}1089 '''Access''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Electric Railway|Q11417705|WD=Q11417705}} (Wakayama Electric Railway) {{ill|lt=Wakayama Electric Railway Kishigawa Line|Q6416586|WD=Q6416586|lt=Kishigawa Line}} {{ill|lt=Naemiya Station|Q11504968|WD=Q11504968}} (about 12 minutes walk) * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Bus|Q11417290|WD=Q11417290}} {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama Station|Q1044107|WD=Q1044107}} From the east exit, Narukami Line 90/94 Narukami Jutaku-mae (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * {{ill|lt=Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184}} * Narukami Shell Midden - Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |Foundation = (legendary) 6th {{ill|lt=Emperor Koan|Q312821|WD=Q312821}} |Main shrine = {{ill|lt=Flowered construction|Q210684|WD=Q210684}}2 buildings |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 12th|Q2920|WD=Q2920}} |Shinto ritual =|Map = {{Location map|Japan Wakayama|label=Narujinja|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=220}} }} {{List of coordinates}} [[File:Naru-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Narukami Shrine'' (Narujinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}{{ill|lt=Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|WD=Q17209155|lt=Narukami}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}) and {{ill|lt=Modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old shrine rank}} is {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined God === The following three deities are currently enshrined. . * '''{{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhiko Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhime no Mikoto}}''' (Hayaakitsuhime Mikoto) - Left shrine (facing the right shrine). * '''{{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Tentadama no Mikoto}}''' (Amenofutodama no Mikoto) - Right shrine (facing left shrine). {{ill|lt=Ibe-san|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}}Ancestor God. The two deities enshrined are the gods of Mito (the gods of the port). As far back as the {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}), in the "Nanki Shrine Record", it appears that the two deities, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, are enshrined {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. However, the earlier "Nanki Meishoshi" states that it is unknown, and it is thought that the enshrined deity was unknown in the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}}{{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Later, in ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}},'' he questioned the theory that two gods were enshrined, Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime, since there was only one deity enshrined at Narukin Shrine in the ``Engishiki,'' and advocated the theory that Tentadama-no-Mikoto was the enshrined deity.{{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. This speculation is based on the theory that the area around this area is compared to the area of ``Ibe-go'' that appears in ``{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}'', and in the same text, it is said that a person lived in this area. {jalink|Ibe clan|Q8189229}} (Kiibe clan) is said to have enshrined its ancestral deity, Tenta Tama no Mikoto {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In addition, the same article states that when Naru Shrine, which had been in ruins, was rebuilt in 4 ({{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902}}), two deities were considered: Hayaakitsuhiko and Hayaakitsuhime. However, Kii-Ibe is not a family of Inebekushi whose ancestor is Tenta Tama-no-Mikoto, but a family of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kikokuzō|UNKNOWN}}, and {{ill|lt=Hikosa Chi-no-Mikoto|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}} is its ancestor deity, according to ``{{ill|lt=Kogo Shui|Q531555|WD=Q531555}}.'' Also, the fourth book of ``Mochizuki'' and ``Sho Keiki,'' which is another name for the founder of Kikuni-zukuri, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takuma Tama no Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}. 's father is said to be a Narukami ({{ill|lt=Raijin|Q12808082|WD=Q12808082}}) called {{ill|lt=Raijin|Q12808082|WD=Q12808082}}, and in the Otomo clan genealogy in "Furuya family family history" and "Kamibetsu genealogy," Tenrai no Mikoto is said to be ``Narukami Shrine (Koreya), Nagusa district, Kii province.''Keninobu Nakata "Shinbetsu Genealogy", {{ill|lt=Tokyo National Museum|Q653433|WD=Q653433}} Digital Library. .Based on the Tentaitama no Mikoto theory, in {{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|WD=Q234510}}8 ({{ill|lt=1996|Q2070|WD=Q2070}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tentadama no Mikoto Shrine|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|WD=Q131287}}{{ill|lt=Kashihara City|Q496319|WD=Q496319}}Ibe Town), the spirit of Tentadama no Mikoto was once again solicited and enshrined in the main shrine.. === History === === Founding === According to the shrine biography cited in "Ki Meisho Zue", around the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Koan|Q312821|WD=Q312821}} (sixth generation), the god {{ill|lt=Ha It is said that there was an oracle to enshrine Yaakitsuhiko Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto}}, and Naru-jinja Shrine was founded as a result of this enshrinement. The company name ``Naru'' is said to be a name related to a river, as it is assumed in ``{{ill|lt=Kii Zoku Fudoki|Q11604964|WD=Q11604964}}'' that it means water flowing from a valley called ``Narumizu'' or ``Narutaki''. {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Gods)|1986}}On the other hand, {{ill|lt=Shohei (Japan)|Q1324874|WD=Q1324874|lt=Shohei}} ({{ill|lt=931|Q24789|WD=Q24789}}-{{ill|lt=938|Q24794|WD=Q24794}}) link|Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542}}", you can see the entry "Inbe" as {{ill|lt=go|Q130458414|WD=Q130458414}} of Nagusa District.. This is believed to be near Inbe, Wakayama City, south of Narukin Shrine, and the area is said to have been the {{ill|lt=honkan|Q846706|WD=Q846706|lt=honkanchi}} of {{ill|lt=Ibe clan|Q8189229|WD=Q8189229}} (Kii Inbe) in Kii. Nagusa District, Ibe-go section, Kobe-go section. . Also, in the same book, ``Nabe'' appears next to ``Ibe'', but this is a misprint of ``Kobe'', and is thought to mean ``Ibe Kobe'', that is, the god of Inbe (a private house attached to a shrine). Relatedly, in the ``Shinshokakuchofusho'' {{ill|lt=Daido (Japan)|Q1373191|WD=Q1373191|lt=Daido}} 1st year ({{ill|lt=806|Q30021|WD=Q30021}}), there are mentions of 10 of the 20 households of Inabe deity in Kobe . It is certain that Kobe was located at Naru-jinja Shrine from the description of ``50 bunches of sake rice, divine tax'' in the Shijiki-sai Ainosai article of the ``Engishiki'', so it is thought that these Inabe-Kobe were attached to Naru-jinja Shrine, and Naru-jinja Shrine was enshrined as the guardian deity of Kii Inabe. It is also assumed that these Kobes were distributed around Narukami, Wakayama City, where Narukami Shrine is located .In addition, in "{{ill|lt=Kogo shui|Q531555|WD=Q531555}}", Kiibe is a descendant of {{ill|lt=Hikosato no Mikoto|Q135327097|WD=Q135327097}}, and is the ruler of Nagusa District. It is written that he lived in Kigo and Arakago (neither of which have "Wamyosho") and was responsible for paying tribute of lumber and building palaces and shrines. . === Outline === In national history, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), there is an article about the promotion of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} from the 8th grade of Jugoi to the 8th grade of Jushii (Junior 4th rank and lower rank) in the first year ({{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}}, {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|lt=Nagusa District|Q11415547|WD=Q11415547}} has "Narujin Shrine Meishin University It will be listed at {{ill|lt=Meishin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}} as ``Monthly Ainame Shinnai'' and {{ill|lt=Monthly Festival|Q11516161|WD=Q11516161}} and {{ill|lt=Ainamesai|Q11581944|WD=Q11581944}}・It is written that it was deposited in {{ill|lt=Heihaku|Q11481885|WD=Q11481885}} at {{ill|lt=Ninamesai|Q11501518|WD=Q11501518}}{{Sfn|Narukinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. There are many famous shrines in Kii Province, but the ones that received dedications at the three festivals are Narukinja Shrine, {{ill|lt=Maezen Shrine, Kunikake Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184|lt=Maejin Shrine}}, {{ja There were only four shrines: link|Mae-mae Jingu/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|Kunikake Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Itaukeso Shrine|Q11379271|WD=Q11379271}}. Regarding these four companies, please refer to "{{ill|lt=Reishūka|Q11378321|WD=Q11378321}}" Jingirei"Reishūkai" Volume 7 (Jingirei) Nakafuyujo Kamiusaōsai. Also, there is a description that it was listed at Hoheisha Shrine on the occasion of the Aijo Festival on Kami U no Hi in November as ``Mae, Kokukensu, Itaukeso, Narukami'' {{Sfn|Narujinsha (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.In the storage rice book of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341}}3 ({{ill|lt=1048|Q19359|WD=Q19359}}), 340 steps of Narukami Shrine rice fields can be seen {{Sfn|Narukin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'', it is written as ``Shoichii Naru-Okami'' {{Sfn|Naru Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} In 13 ({{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|WD=Q6736}}), {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|WD=Q187550}}'s {{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721}} {{ill|lt=Ota Castle (Ki Province)|Q10941625|WD=Q10941625|lt=Ota Castle}}It is said that during the water attack, the surrounding area, including Narukin Shrine, was greatly devastated. The shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}4 ({{ill|lt=1719|Q6902|WD=Q6902}}), and five Kanda shrines were donated {{Sfn|Narujinja (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In the 40th year of the Meiji era ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines, the nearby Shikinai shrines Kenmaon Shrine and Katsuchi Shrine were enshrined within the shrine grounds. === God level === * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} January 27, 1985 ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from Jufifth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Rank, 8th Class ("{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}") - The notation is "Narukami". * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii (``{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as "Narutokami". === Precincts === The main hall consists of two halls. {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|WD=Q11059539|lt=Tentadama no Mikoto}} is enshrined in the right shrine (facing the left shrine), and {{ill|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko/Hayaakitsuhime|Q11326733|WD=Q11326733|lt=Hayaakitsuhiko-no-Mikoto/Hayaakitsuhime-no-Mikoto}} is enshrined in the left shrine. Katsuchi Shrine: Shikinai-sha (small), former village-sha |Soken = |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=October 12th|Q2920|WD=Q2920}} (Both shrines) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} |Shinto ritual = }} === Kenmaon Shrine === ``Katamaoto Shrine'' (Katamanoto Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukin Shrine. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kotsuchi Shrine. The following deities are enshrined: * Kamiadaka Ashitsuhime no Mikoto {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} *: Another name for {{ill|lt=Konohana Sakuyabime|Q1781862|WD=Q1781862|lt=Konohana Sakuyahime no Mikoto}}. In national history, {{ill|lt=Jōkan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} In the first year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}, he was conferred {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}}, which is the upper rank of Jugoi, and in the seventh year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=865 In 1983|Q30350|WD=Q30350}}), it was ranked as a government shrine, and in the 8th year of Jogan ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), there is a record that it was conferred the rank of Junior Fourth Rank and Lower.{{Sfn|Kanshin Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}."{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} is {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{jalink |Nagusa District|Q11415547}} is described as "Kanmaon Shrine" and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}}, and in "{{ill|lt=Kinokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}" it is listed as "Shoichii Shrine". ``Arimaon Okami'' is compared to Kenmaon Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Relatedly, in "{{ill|lt=Japanese name Ruijusho|Q2702542|WD=Q2702542}}", "Arima" is mentioned as {{ill|lt=go|Q130458414|WD=Q130458414}} of Nagusa District, and it is determined that it is near Narukami, Wakayama City. . Kenmaon Shrine was originally located approximately 300 meters northeast of Naru Shrine {{Sfn|Kanmaon Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsune Shrine Kyoho Kotatsu'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{ill|lt=Kyoho|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}}9 ({{ill|lt=1724|Q6926|WD=Q6926}}) {{Sfn|Katsune Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.{{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} April 7th ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|WD=Q7761}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Modern company rating system|UNKNOWN}}, {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi Shrine)|1987|p=58-59}} listed as {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. In 1907 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. Later, a branch spirit was summoned to the shrine in Wakayama City, and the shrine building was rebuilt (currently Kenma-on Shrine, {{Coord|34|12|50.79|N|135|12|9.45|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Kanma-on Shrine (descendant shrine)}}). The shrine is as follows. * Records of {{ill|lt=Divine Rank|Q11591025|WD=Q11591025}} dedication in the history of the Six Kingdoms (all written as "Kanma Ongami") ** {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} May 26th, 1st year ({{ill|lt=859|Q23897|WD=Q23897}}), from upper 6th rank to upper junior fifth rank (``{{ill|lt=Japan Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}'') ** On May 17, 7th year of Jogan era ({{ill|lt=865 years|Q30350|WD=Q30350}}), he was listed as a government shrine (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') ** Leap March 13, 8th year of the Jogan era ({{ill|lt=866|Q23872|WD=Q23872}}), from the top of the fifth rank to the bottom of the fourth rank (``Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku'') * After Six Kingdoms History ** Shoichii ("{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Namecho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}") - Written as "Arimaon Okami". === Katsuchi Shrine === '''Katsuchi Shrine'' (Katsuchi Shrine) is a shrine within the precincts of Narukinja Shrine. {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=Old company rank}} is {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. Currently, it is enshrined together with Kenmaon Shrine. The following deities are enshrined. * {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|WD=Q1640658|lt=Encounter with Tsuchi no Mikoto}} (Kagutsuchi no Mikoto) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine/Kanmaon Shrine (Shikiuchi)|1987|p=58-59}} [[File:Naru-jinja Sakamatsu-sha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Sakamatsu Shrine (Narukami, Wakayama City)
The former enshrinement site of Katsuchi Shrine. }}]] "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho}} is {{ill|lt=Kiokuni|Q864053|WD=Q864053}}{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nakusa District|UNKNOWN}}, it is written as ``Katsuchi Shrine'' and is listed as {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikiuchi|UNKNOWN}}, but in some manuscripts it is annotated as ``Narukami Sue Shrine'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. Also, in ``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Jinmyocho|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'' it is written as ``Junior Fourth Rank Upper Katsuchijin'' {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}.Kotsuchi Shrine was originally located about 200 meters northeast of Naruko Shrine. rd|34|13|44.05|N|135|12|52.10|E|region:JP-30_type:landmark|name=Gakamatsu Shrine (formerly Katsuchi Shrine)}}). {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}} It is said that it was in ruins in the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963}} due to the military fire of the {{ill|lt=Kishū Conquest|Q10855721|WD=Q10855721}}. At his place of birth, there is a monument erected as ``Katsuchijin Keian Kotora'', which appears to be inscribed in the year {{ill|lt=Keian|Q1146135|WD=Q1146135}}3 ({{ill|lt=1650|Q6951|WD=Q6951}}) {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. {{ill|lt=Enkyo|Q1183728|WD=Q1183728}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1746|Q7589|WD=Q7589}}) "Nanki Jinja Roku" describes Kotsuchi Shrine as "Muhoden erected stone monument" {{Sfn|Katsuchi Shrine Ruins (Heibonsha)|1983|p=370}}. In April of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}}6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), he was ranked as {{ill|lt=murasha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} in the {{ill|lt=modern company ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}}. In 1907 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|WD=Q2048}}), it was relocated to the precincts of Narukin Shrine in accordance with the ordinance to enshrine shrines. The shrine is as follows. * After Six Kingdoms History** Junior Fourth Rank Upper (``{{ill|lt=Kiokuni Shinto Name Book|Q17210008|WD=Q17210008}}'') - Written as ``Katsuchijin''. === Others === [[File:Naru-jinja keidaisha.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Shinuchisha}}]] [[File:Naru-jinja Narutake-jinja.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Narutake Shrine}}]] '''Precinct shrine''' * Amaterasu Daijingu ShrineThe description of other shrines is from [http://wakayama-jinjacho.or.jp/jdb/sys/user/GetWjtTbl.php?JinjyaNo=1056 Narukin Shrine] (Wakayama Prefectural Shrine Agency). * Kasugasha * Yume Shrine * Inarisha ``Kingaisha'''' * Narutake Shrine *: Another name is "Tsubo Gozen" . It is enshrined in front of the torii gate of Naru Shrine. The enshrined deity, Narumu Daimyojin, is said to be the fourth princess of King Kija of {{ill|lt=Baekje|Q28428|WD=Q28428}}, and is said to have come from Baekje to Kii Province around the 5th century and introduced sake brewing. Currently, only the stone shrine remains, but in ancient times there was a shrine. === Local information === '''Location''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|Q131314|WD=Q131314}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama City|Q200747|WD=Q200747}}{{ill|lt=Narukami (Wakayama City)|Q17209155|WD=Q17209155|lt=Narukami}}1089 '''Access''' * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Electric Railway|Q11417705|WD=Q11417705}} (Wakayama Electric Railway) {{ill|lt=Wakayama Electric Railway Kishigawa Line|Q6416586|WD=Q6416586|lt=Kishigawa Line}} {{ill|lt=Naemiya Station|Q11504968|WD=Q11504968}} (about 12 minutes walk) * {{ill|lt=Wakayama Bus|Q11417290|WD=Q11417290}} {{ill|lt=West Japan Railway Company|Q502125|WD=Q502125|lt=JR}}{{ill|lt=Wakayama Station|Q1044107|WD=Q1044107}} From the east exit, Narukami Line 90/94 Narukami Jutaku-mae (about 5 minutes walk) '''Surroundings''' * {{ill|lt=Maezen Shrine/Kokukake Shrine|Q888184|WD=Q888184}} * Narukami Shell Midden -
